title: Agurk Salat (Cucumber Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large, green cucumbers
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Peel and cut into as thin slices as possible.
2. Place on a plate with a large pinch of salt between the slices and a fork placed over them.
3. Let stand for a few hours, then press firmly between the hands so no salt water follows.
4. Place in a dish and add vinegar and pepper.
5. If you wish to prepare them immediately before the meal, you can sprinkle salt on the slices, place them between two hot plates and press them firmly so the salt dissolves around them, then drain and serve.

title: Agurker (Pickled Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- small cucumbers
- salt
- cold water
- grape leaves
- alum (a piece the size of a pea per 2 gallons)
- vinegar (cider vinegar)
- sugar
- black pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Select small cucumbers no larger than a small finger. Pack them in a crock or stone jar with generous salt between the layers. Cover the top completely with salt and pour enough cold water over to cover the cucumbers. Place a plate over them and weight it down with a clean cloth to keep them submerged. They can stand in the brine for a whole month, stirring from the bottom every other day. After 48 hours, the cucumbers can be removed if desired.
2. To pickle: drain the brine and remove any soft cucumbers. Place the remaining cucumbers in fresh water for 24 hours, then change the water and let stand another 24 hours. Line the bottom of the pickling vessel with grape leaves and arrange the cucumbers on them, sprinkling powdered alum between the layers. Fill with cold water and cover with more grape leaves. Heat gently without boiling for 4-5 hours.
3. For the vinegar: to 1 gallon of cider vinegar add 1 cup sugar, 3 dozen black peppercorns, the same number of nutmegs, half as much allspice, and a little mace. Boil for 5 minutes. Pour the hot vinegar over the cucumbers in a stone crock. Cover with a plate. Two days later, reheat the vinegar and pour it over again. Repeat this process three times at intervals of 2, 4, and 6 days. Place a plate that fits inside the crock over the cucumbers and tie a cloth over the top. They can be eaten after 6 weeks.

title: Agurker med Løg (Cucumbers with Onion)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 large yellow cucumbers
- salt

## Directions

1. Scrape, slice, and hollow out the cucumbers and place them in cold well water for 24 hours.
2. Remove them, dry them, and sprinkle them well with salt.
3. Lay them tightly together and let them stand for another 24 hours.
4. Remove them, place them on a sieve to drain, and dry them.

title: Almond Porridge (Mandel-Grød)
body:
## Ingredients

- One half pound of rice flour
- 2 quarts of milk
- Finely chopped blanched almonds
- Salt
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Stir the flour into some of the cold milk, adding it bit by bit so it does not lump.
2. Place the rest on the fire.
3. When it boils, combine the unstirred flour with a bit of the warm milk and pour it into the porridge with strong stirring back and forth.
4. Simmer for 5 minutes.
5. Add finely sliced blanched almonds and salt at the end.
6. It is cooked in a good quarter hour and eaten with sugar, butter and cinnamon.
7. This porridge is particularly pleasant to eat cold; then use a little less milk and a few drops of vanilla.
8. Pour it into a mold, from which it is turned out and served with fruit juice over it.
9. As a warm porridge without butter and almonds it is good for children.

title: Another Simple Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart cold milk
- 1 tablespoonful vanilla
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 10 beaten egg whites (according to instruction)
- 10 egg yolks
- 1 pint cream
- A little salt

## Directions

1. Place 1 quart cold milk over heat with 1 tablespoonful vanilla and 1 tablespoon sugar
2. Cook 10 beaten egg whites and strain through a sieve
3. Boil the milk and stir it little by little into 10 egg yolks that have been beaten white and mixed with 1 pint cream
4. Place this on heat and stir until it begins to boil, then remove it
5. Add a little salt and stir until it is cold
6. Lay the cooked whites over the cream

title: Appelsin Marmelade (Orange Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges (pound for pound with sugar)
- Sugar (pound for pound with oranges)
- Water
- Butter (optional, small pieces)

## Directions

1. Pound for pound, half of the oranges are peeled and the peels are cut in strips, and cooked in three changes of warm water until they are tender; after which they are set aside.
2. On the other oranges the peels are rubbed off; the inside thick white skin is removed and discarded; all oranges are cut into 4 pieces and the seeds removed; next the fruit is cut into very small pieces, and all the juice that comes from them, without pressing them, is poured over the sugar; this is kept until the sugar is melted, and if the fruit is not very juicy [continue cooking process as indicated by incomplete text].

title: Appelsin Marmelade (Orange Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges and cut the peel into strips, and cook in three changes of water until tender; then set aside.
2. On the remaining oranges remove the peel; the inner white skin is taken off and discarded; all oranges are cut into 4 pieces and the seeds removed; then cut the fruit into very small pieces, and all the juice that comes from them, without pressing them, is poured over the sugar; this is heated until the sugar is melted, and if the fruit is not very juicy, add a very little water.
3. Cook and skim for five minutes; then add the cooked peel and cook another ten minutes and finally the diced fruit and clean peel, and the whole cooks now two minutes longer.
4. When it is cold, pour it into small glasses or pots, cover with brandy paper and seal.

title: Apple Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- Cider (1 kettle)
- Apples (various kinds, or better if sour)
- Sugar (scant 1 pound per 1 pound apples)
- Nutmeg and cinnamon (optional, for flavoring)

## Directions

1. A kettle full of cider is boiled until one-third of it is boiled away.
2. Various kinds of apples can be used as long as they are juicy, but it does not matter as much if they are sour; peel them and cut into small pieces, removing the cores, and put as many in the kettle as the cider can stand over.
3. It cooks slowly while you stir often with a flat spoon or wooden utensil, and when the apples are quite tender, remove them with a perforated skimming spoon or sieve, taking care not to get juice with them.
4. Now put fresh apples in the cider, as many as it can hold, and treat them as before.
5. Then remove the pot from the stove, and the pulp is poured into a large jar where it is allowed to stand until the next day, under a closed lid.
6. Then it is put back in the kettle and cooked, constantly stirring, until it is like a soft paste and quite brown.
7. It can be flavored according to taste with nutmeg and cinnamon.
8. It keeps all winter in stone jars, in a dry, cold place.

title: Barley Soup (Barley Gruel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts water
- 1 pound coarse barley grains
- sugar
- whole cinnamon stick
- lemon zest
- salted juice

## Directions

1. Set 2 quarts of water on the fire, when it boils, stir in 1 pound coarse barley grains; when they are completely softened, strain the soup through a fairly fine sieve so that no grain follows along, and set it again over the fire with sugar to taste, a little whole cinnamon and lemon zest; when it has come to a boil, it is poured into the tureen, served with salted juice and eaten with toasted bread.

title: Beef Cakes (Kød melet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds Round Beef
- 6 eggs
- 5 or 6 soda crackers
- Butter
- Minced beef suet
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Scrape and mince the beef finely, crumble the soda crackers finely, add a little butter, a little minced beef suet, pepper and salt.
2. Mix all of this together and form two small loaves, which are rolled in crackers and baked for one hour.
3. When they are cold, slice them.

title: Beef Heart (Hjertesoppa)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef heart
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg yolk
- Onion
- Catsup
- Parsley
- Celery root
- Cayenne pepper
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Wash the heart well and cut it into quarters, about an inch long, add water so it stands over the pieces and cook for ten minutes.
2. Salt the water a little to draw out the blood and skim very carefully as the scum rises.
3. Take out the heart and strain the soup, mince the heart finely and put it back in the soup with a sliced onion, a large tablespoon of catsup, minced parsley, a little minced celery root, a little cayenne pepper and a good lump of butter.
4. Cook now until the meat is very tender, then stir a tablespoon of flour into it, dissolved in a little cold water.
5. Let it boil up once and serve straight.

title: Beef or Steak Sliced (Bifsteg eller Stegen)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef or steak
- Flour
- Eggs
- Butter
- Milk
- Salt
- Sugar
- Bread

## Directions

1. Cut the beef or steak into thin, fine slices
2. Dip in a batter made of 1 pound flour, 3 beaten eggs, 2 ounces melted butter, 1 pint milk, a little sugar and salt
3. Roast on the stove in clarified butter
4. Serve with apple compote, starch compote, rhubarb compote or other vegetables

title: Beef Ragout (Ragout af Kogekød)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Onion
- Anchovies
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Lemon juice
- Cognac
- Beef or meat bouillon
- Beef broth
- Red wine
- Potatoes
- French bread

## Directions

1. Brown 2 ounces butter, add 1 beaten egg, then 2 anchovies scraped from bones and chopped, a little salt, pepper or parsley, juice of half a lemon, 1 teaspoon Cognac and 1 tablespoon flour
2. Add beef broth, 1 pint bouillon, strong meat broth and about 1 pint red wine (optional)
3. Simmer the whole dish for a good quarter hour
4. When serving, place browned potatoes on top or arrange slices of roasted French bread on the plate and cover with the ragout

title: Beef Soup (Oxtail Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oxtails
- Water
- Salt and pepper
- Small onions
- Celery roots (to be blanched separately)
- Parsley and celery
- Thyme
- Bay leaves
- Butter
- A piece of white sugar

## Directions

1. Rinse the oxtails very well in cold water and let them stand for a while in fresh water.
2. Bring them to the boil slowly with even, uniform heat; skim well as they cook.
3. Remove the lid only when the soup foams.
4. Do not dilute the soup, but if necessary, add only boiling water after all has been removed.
5. Strain the soup through a fine sieve; if it is not very clear, place a cloth inside.
6. Let it cool slightly before straining; remove the fat and pour carefully back into the pot so the sediment does not come with it.
7. When the soup boils again, skim once more and remove all foam carefully.
8. The vegetables (small onions and celery roots) are best cooked separately in water or light soup so the soup remains clearer and does not lose flavor.
9. When serving, a piece of white sugar may be added for pleasant flavor.
10. This portion requires about 3 hours of cooking.

title: Beef Steak (Beefsteak)
body:
## Ingredients

- Porterhouse beef
- Butter
- Salt

## Directions

1. The meat for beef steak should be a Porterhouse.
2. The sinew is cut from the sides; it is best to get a thick piece, which you then beat well.
3. Then place it on the grill and cook it over clear coals from hardwood.
4. When you think it is cooked enough on one side, turn it.
5. You must not let it become too dry; it should be red inside.
6. When it is done, place it on a platter, lay cold butter and salt over it, and let it rest a moment in the oven.

title: Beef Tea (Beef Tea)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound lean beef
- 1 pint cold water

## Directions

1. Chop the beef finely
2. Place it in 1 pint of cold water in a lined pot
3. Set over heat in the same water and bring slowly to a boil
4. Simmer for 15 to 30 minutes, depending on available time
5. Strain it and season lightly with salt. Since this diet is chiefly intended for the sick, it may be that there is not time to let it stand for an hour in water, and therefore less time will do, but the longer the beef can lie in cold water beforehand, the more nourishing extract will be drawn out.

title: Beste Marmelade (Best Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- Water
- Sugar (scant 1 pound sugar per 1 pound fruit)
- Apple juice (juice of 2 apples per 3 pounds fruit, optional)

## Directions

1. Peel and slice the quinces into pieces with the stones removed; cook the peelings, stones and cores in scant water under a lid; when they have cooked long enough to draw out all the juice, strain the water through a thick cloth.
2. When this water is approximately cold, put the quinces in a clay pot and pour the water over them, and it cooks, stirring and mashing the fruit with a spoon, gradually as it becomes soft.
3. This gives a pleasant flavor if you add the juice of 2 apples to every 3 pounds of fruit.
4. When it is stirred to a soft pulp, stir in scant 1 pound of sugar for every pound of fruit; cook for another 10 minutes, stirring.
5. Then remove it, and when cold, put it in small jars or glasses with brandy paper over.

title: Blommer No. 1 (Plums Preserved No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fully ripe plums
- Vinegar (half a gill per pound of plums)
- Sugar (3 ounces per pound of plums)
- Cinnamon stick
- Cloves

## Directions

1. The plums must be fully ripe
2. Dry them, prick with a pointed toothpick or needle and place in a crock
3. Pour over them half a gill vinegar per pound of plums and let stand until the next day
4. Pour off the vinegar onto the plums with 3 ounces sugar per pound, place on the fire, and when it is just melted, add the plums and turn them a couple of times around so those that were at the bottom come to the top
5. When it begins to boil, pour it into the crock, leave uncovered until the next day, pour it into another crock so the juice can come to all the plums, and the next day put the whole thing back on the fire, but remove it as soon as it begins to boil
6. Two days later set it back on the fire, and now cook the plums until they are not sour by the stones, usually in 1.5 hours; then place them in a sieve so the juice can drain off
7. Cook the juice a good quarter hour, or until it becomes thick, with whole cinnamon and cloves
8. Pour it into a crock and when it is cold, place the plums in it, though not too many, so there can be sufficient juice around them
9. Seal them the next day
10. If there is more juice than can be with the plums, pour this into bottles
11. If one wishes to use these plums for roasts, it is good to add some fresh currant leaves

title: Blommer No. 2 (Plums Preserved No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums (for each pound, take 0.75 pound sugar)
- Vinegar (0.5 gill per pound)
- Water (1 tablespoon per pound)
- Cinnamon stick
- Cloves

## Directions

1. For each pound of plums take 0.75 pound sugar, which is cooked on the fire with 0.5 gill vinegar and 1 tablespoon water; when it is melted, pour it over the plums, which are first dried, pricked and placed in a crock
2. After 3-4 days, during which the plums are poured into another crock each day so the juice can come around them, place them over the fire where they cook until they are no longer sour by the stones, about 1.5 hours; then place them in a coarse sieve and from there in a crock, which is filled halfway with them
3. Cook the juice with cinnamon and cloves in a quarter hour or until it is thick
4. Pour it warm over the plums, which are sealed the next day
5. The plums can also be preserved in the same way with 0.75 pound sugar and 0.5 gill vinegar per pound, but then they have less juice

title: Blueberry Soup (Blaabaerssuppe)
body:
## Ingredients

- blueberries
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Made the same way as the preceding dish, but using blueberries instead of strawberries.

title: Blåbær (Huckle-Berries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound ripe blueberries
- 1 pound powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Place over fire with powdered sugar and cook for approximately one quarter hour; then strain the berries through a sieve.
2. Cook the juice for a quarter hour with cinnamon, skim and pour hot over the berries, which are covered the next day with paper dipped in vinegar and tied.

title: Boiled Trout with Oyster Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds trout
- salt
- water
- 1 pint oysters
- 1 ounce butter
- 1 ounce flour
- 1 egg yolk
- half teaspoon salt
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and boil the trout in salted water according to instructions.
2. Once the trout is done, remove the head from the fire and set it aside.
3. Meanwhile, set a pint of oysters over the fire to cook in their own sauce.
4. Once the oysters are cooked, strain the sauce and set it back over the fire to boil.
5. Melt one ounce of butter over the fire in a frying pan, stir in one ounce of flour.
6. Thicken the sauce with one beaten egg yolk, half a teaspoon of salt and a pinch of cayenne pepper.
7. Add the oysters to the sauce and serve in a sauce bowl.

title: Boston Brown Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cent yeast, compressed
- 6 potatoes, mashed together with 3 cups flour
- 1 quart warm water
- 2 tablespoons fat
- 2 tablespoons brown sugar
- 1 quart cornmeal
- 2 quarts cornmeal
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1/2 cup molasses

## Directions

1. Make a dough consisting of: 2 cent compressed yeast, 6 potatoes mashed together with 3 cups flour, 1 quart warm water, 2 tablespoons fat and 2 tablespoons brown sugar
2. Mix well and set overnight in a warm place
3. When light, sift in: 1 quart cornmeal, 2 quarts cornmeal, 1 tablespoon salt, 1 teaspoon baking powder and 1/2 cup molasses
4. Mix to a very soft dough, adding more warm water gradually if needed
5. Knead well and let rise 6 to 7 hours
6. Knead again and divide into loaves, place in well greased, deep, round bread pans
7. Let rise again for about an hour in a warm place, then bake in a moderately warm oven for about 3 to 4 hours
8. If setting this in the oven early in the day when the kitchen is small, it can stay in the oven the whole night

title: Braised Calf's Breast (Blanguet af Kaltekød)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds calf's breast
- Cold water
- White pepper
- Salt
- Vegetables (carrots, turnips)
- Onion
- Cloves
- Butter
- Flour
- Nutmeg
- Egg white
- Egg yolk
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the calf's breast into small pieces and place in sufficient cold water to cover them; add half a teaspoon of white pepper, a teaspoon of salt, a good bunch of vegetables, half a scraped carrot, a peeled turnip and an onion in which 3 cloves are stuck.
2. Bring to a gentle boil and skim carefully as long as scum rises, and cook until the meat is tender; then strain it, return the soup to the fire and the meat is washed in cold water.
3. In the meantime make a white sauce by melting a little butter and stirring flour into it and gradually adding 1½ pints of the soup; add a little more pepper and salt if necessary, and half a teaspoon of grated nutmeg.
4. When the sauce has boiled up, thicken it with an egg white and two small egg yolks stirred in, add the meat and cook a couple of minutes while stirring occasionally.
5. It can be served with a tablespoon of minced parsley scattered over it after it has been placed on a warm dish.

title: Braised Calf's Feet (Kalvehorn)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cold calf's feet
- Butter
- Catsup
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vegetable
- Prepared mustard
- Warm water
- Onion

## Directions

1. Cut thin slices of cold calf's feet and place them in a tin pot set in a pot of boiling water.
2. Over the meat put a sauce made of 3 tablespoons melted butter, a tablespoon of catsup, a teaspoon of vinegar, a little salt and pepper, a tablespoon of vegetable, a teaspoon of prepared mustard and a little warm water.
3. Place an onion on top and it is ready in half an hour when you are sure that the water in the outer pot is at a gentle boil.

title: Brosme Trout (Brosme Trout)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brosme fish
- Flour
- Salt
- Parsley
- Butter or lard

## Directions

1. Clean as usual; it must be quite dry before it is rolled in flour and fried in butter, clarified or lard.
2. The lard must be boiling hot, so it fries quickly to a nice light brown color, and it must be taken up the moment it is done.

title: Brown Bread Soup (Bread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small piece of sweet bread, about 1 pound by weight
- Water
- 6 large pieces of celery
- Beef stock
- Small white carrots (cut into pieces)
- Beef balls (meatballs)

## Directions

1. Rinse the sweet bread and cook it in barely enough water together with 6 large pieces of celery until they easily fall apart.
2. Strain the whole mixture with beef stock, cook a little, rub through a colander, then through a sieve.
3. Set it back on the fire with some finely cut white carrots.
4. When it boils, place small beef balls on top.
5. In the absence of beef balls, the soup may be eaten with toasted bread.
6. Alternatively, brown a piece of butter in the pot, add 2 tablespoons full of finely cut carrots and 1 tablespoon of onion, and fry well until light brown.
7. Then add the soup gradually, simmer gently until the carrots fall apart, rub through a colander with more soup, finally through a sieve, and finish with cut white carrots and small beef balls.

title: Brown Cream Sauce (Brun Gløde Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- egg yolk
- flour
- egg yolk
- cream

## Directions

1. Take equal parts butter and egg yolk for brown sauce. The butter is browned in a pan and a tablespoon of flour is browned in it. This is made with a couple of tablespoons of egg yolk. When this boils, the cream is added and stirred hard until the sauce boils. If you have no egg yolk, then do not use flour either, but then the cream must be good. If it should happen that it separates, a little more cream is taken and if it becomes too thick from that, it is remedied with soda. This sauce is for veal and poultry and can also be used for beef, goose and duck. For poultry one should avoid soda.

title: Browned Egg-Yolk Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 4 raw egg yolks
- 0.5 pound white or butter sugar
- 0.5 pound unwashed butter
- 0.5 pound flour

## Directions

1. Beat hard-boiled yolks and mix well with raw yolks, sugar and butter and flour. Mix to a stiff dough from which small pieces are taken, dipped in flour, rolled with the teeth and formed into small wreaths which are smeared with egg white on one side, strewed with white, coarse, soft sugar and baked light brown.

title: Brunet Hviidkaal (Browned Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Sugar
- Cabbage
- Salt
- Egg

## Directions

1. A good spoonful of butter is browned, and sugar is stirred into it; the cabbage must be sliced very finely and laid in it over a gentle fire under a covered pot until it is tender, then salt is added.
2. It is good, if one has it, to stir a little egg into the cabbage when it is finished. Boiled cabbage is best browned this way.

title: Bræ'dn med Hvosike til Kød (Quince Sauce for Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces (ripe, 2 pounds)
- Water (1 pint)
- Vinegar (1 pint)
- Sugar (1.5 pounds, good, crushed)
- Additional water if needed

## Directions

1. The quinces should be very ripe, peel and cut into 4 to 6 pieces according to their size; cut away the stone, weigh them and scatter them with small pieces of butter and scraped horseradish.
2. For 2 pounds of quinces take 1 pint of water, 1 pint of vinegar and 1.5 pounds of good crushed sugar.
3. If the quinces are not completely covered, take out more of this mixture.
4. Put it together on the stove until the quinces are tender, then remove them and put in a tureen.
5. Cook the sauce until it is perfectly smooth, and pour it over the quinces which are tied up the next day.

title: Brød Kager (Bread Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 cents toast, or as much old bread as that equals the same amount
- 1 pint milk
- 3 eggs
- 3 tablespoons melted butter
- lemon peel
- rubber sugar
- a little salt
- a little coriander if desired

## Directions

1. For 5 cents toast, or as much old bread as that equals the same amount, it is finely chopped and mixed with 1 pint milk with which it stands for an hour
2. Then it is stirred well with 3 eggs, 3 tablespoons melted butter, lemon peel, rubber sugar, a little salt, and if desired a little coriander
3. They are fried in clarified and rather long, so they become hard outside
4. Eaten with hot sugar syrup

title: Butter Beans (Den gule Butter-Bean)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Put the beans in boiling water and cook completely tender. Drain the water from them, and they get a little sauce in a little flour and butter, salt and pepper.

title: Calf's Chops (Koltetter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's meat or ham and pork meat
- Butter
- Vegetables or potatoes

## Directions

1. Calf's meat is bought as ribs; the butcher cuts it into chops with a bone in each; from ham meat one must cut two bones to each piece for it to be thick enough, but only one remains adhering.
2. They are eaten with vegetables or potatoes and no other sauce than that which remains on the platter, for which reason some butter is placed there.

title: Calf's Foot Soup (Brown Calf's Foot Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 ounces butter
- 2 large carrots
- 1 parsnip
- 3 onions
- A small bunch of parsley and celery
- A little thyme
- 3 bay leaves
- 1 tablespoon whole pepper
- 1 pint water
- 1 stop (measure) of wine
- 5 pints water (for dilution)
- Salt
- Good beef stock (for finishing)
- 1 pint wine or rum (optional)
- Beef balls (meatballs)

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned calf's feet into pieces.
2. Melt 8 ounces of butter over the fire in a pot until well browned.
3. Add 2 large carrots, 1 parsnip, 3 onions, a small bunch of parsley and celery, a little thyme, and 3 bay leaves, all finely chopped, along with 1 tablespoon of whole pepper.
4. Brown the pepper carefully in the butter without burning it.
5. Simmer for 5 hours, then add 1 pint of water.
6. After this has cooked a while, dilute gradually with 5 pints of water.
7. Add the calf's foot pieces with salt and simmer under cover with regular stirring until the meat is so tender it can easily be removed from the bones with a spoon.
8. Remove the pieces and pour them warm into the tureen while the soup is strained and finished with good stock and optionally 1 pint of wine or rum.
9. If the upper parts of the feet are too large to be in the tureen, cut the meat from the bones and add it to the tureen.

title: Calf's Head (Kallekop)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's head
- salt
- water
- egg
- bread crumbs
- pepper
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Scald, scrape, and clean the head, remove the brain, wash it well and soak it in cold water for a time so the blood can be drawn out
2. Place it in boiling salted water and cook until the bones can be easily removed, then remove with great care
3. Cut off the ears and eyes, remove the palate, cut the tongue and cook it together with the head if desired
4. Cook the head in butter or clarified butter after dipping it in beaten egg and rolling it in bread crumbs mixed with salt and pepper
5. Fry it light brown and serve with brown butter and salad or other garnish along with potatoes

title: Calf's Head, Boiled (Kalvehoovet)
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's head of medium size
- Water
- Salt
- Tongue

## Directions

1. When a cold calf's head of medium size is cleaned well, the tongue is taken out for better cleaning; this and the head, which is skinned, are cooked in water and salt until tender.

title: Calf's Head, Braised (Kalbehoved Stuvning)
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's head
- Butter
- Flour
- Pepper
- Cucumbers or apples
- Bouillon
- Vinegar
- Soda
- Nutmeg or curry
- Salt
- Egg yolk

## Directions

1. Place the calf's head in sufficient boiling salted water and cook until tender, then remove it and untie the binding.
2. Make a brown sauce of browned butter, flour cooked for a time, pepper and cucumbers or apples, which are thinned with bouillon, along with a little vinegar or soda.
3. Give the head a coating in this sauce, then keep it warm on a serving platter and cover with the sauce.
4. If there is not enough head meat, some veal can be cooked with the meat and placed in the middle of the platter with the head around it.

title: Calf's Liver (Kalvelever)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's liver
- salt
- ground pepper
- cloves
- bouillon or egg and water
- butter
- flour
- honey
- egg
- red or white wine

## Directions

1. When the intestines are removed from the liver, place it in several changes of cold water so the blood can be drawn out, then season it with flesh
2. Brown a piece of butter and turn the liver several times in it
3. When it is light brown, add salt, ground pepper, and a couple of whole cloves
4. Add enough bouillon or egg and water so there is enough sauce
5. When the liver is tender, remove it and strain the sauce if needed, return it to the heat and thicken it with flour mixed in egg, soda, or a little cold wine
6. Pour the sauce over the liver
7. It gives good flavor to cook some whole eggs with the liver and serve them around it
8. The wine may be omitted
9. Alternatively, cut the liver into fairly thick pieces and cook on the stove in enough butter and lard or clarified butter to allow it to simmer
10. When the liver is cooked through and placed on a plate, pour off the fat from the pan, add some cold butter, and when this is brown, gradually stir in cold water or egg until the sauce is smooth, then pour over the liver

title: Calf's Meat à l'Anglaise (Kaltekød à l'Anglaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large tablespoon butter
- 2 pounds calf's meat
- Water
- Flour

## Directions

1. Place a large tablespoon of butter over the fire in a pot; when it begins to brown add 2 pounds of calf's meat, which can be simple meat, shoulder or neck, and brown it.
2. Then add enough water so it stands just over the meat, skim as much as necessary; when the meat is tender stir a few tablespoons of flour in good flour paste and with this thicken the sauce.
3. It is an inexpensive and good-tasting dish.

title: Canned Green-Corn (Konservert Majs)
body:
## Ingredients

- corn on the cob
- salt
- melted fat (not lard)

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves and boil the corn until no milky juice comes out when you prick the kernels.
2. Cut the kernels off and pack them in stone crocks in the following order: 3 pounds, one bag salt, one half gallon of dill, then one bag corn, two gallons of dill, and so on until the crock is nearly full.
3. The topmost layer should be one gallon of salt, and over the whole pour melted fat (not lard).
4. Press a bag of clean white paper that fits the crock on top, and bind the crock with paper or better with a pig's bladder.
5. Store in a cold place.
6. Soak overnight before use.
7. If the top layer should become moldy, which may happen, not all the corn is affected, and you only need to remove this layer.

title: Caramel Cake (Karamelkage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 4 tablespoons flour
- 4.5 cups flour
- 5 eggs
- lemon zest and juice from 1 lemon
- 1 tablespoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat together butter and sugar as usual, then add the other ingredients. Beat the whites stiffly and fold in, as well as sifted flour which comes in by itself. Between each layer of cake place a filling made like for cocoa layer cake jelly cake, except cocoa is omitted. On top of each layer spread cold and chopped almonds, about a thumb's distance apart.

title: Carrots (Gulerødber)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- butter
- flour
- milk or cream
- minced parsley
- salt

## Directions

1. If they are young, scraped, old ones are peeled thinly and sliced. They are put in boiling, salted water and cook from half an hour to an hour. Then they are cut into pieces about an inch in diameter; a large spoonful of butter is melted; into this is stirred a spoonful of flour and it is thinned with good milk or cream; into this the carrots are given a seasoning, together with a spoonful of finely minced parsley.

title: Celery Salad (Selleri Salat)
body:
## Ingredients

- equal parts cooked celeriac and celery
- egg yolks
- mustard
- salt
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the celeriac and celery into very small pieces
2. Mix the celeriac and celery well together in a deep bowl
3. Pour the sauce, which has become slightly warm, over them
4. Do not pour the sauce over until the salad is to be served

title: Celery Sauce (Selleri Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- butter
- beef broth
- celery

## Directions

1. Two tablespoons of flour are mixed with a tablespoon full of butter. One pint of boiling beef broth is prepared. The broth and butter are stirred into the flour. Three celery roots cut into small pieces are boiled in water, which is then strained off. After that the celery is put into the flour and it is set over the fire for five to ten minutes while constantly stirring.

title: Cherry Soup (Kirsebærssuppe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cherries
- 1 quart water
- 1 pint water
- whole cinnamon
- cloves
- sugar
- bread rolls or baked bread

## Directions

1. Stone and cook 1 pound cherries with 1 quart water, drain, cook again with 1 pint water and strain into the first drained soup.
2. To clarify the soup, you can place a clean cloth in the sieve and pour the soup through it, after which it is returned to heat with whole cinnamon, cloves and sugar to achieve a good flavor.
3. Serve with bread rolls, which are set on as the soup boils, or with baked bread.
4. You may remove a few berries and cook them in the strained soup.
5. If you want the soup clear, a heaping spoonful of cornstarch is stirred in water or some of the soup while it is cold, mixed in when warm and stirred as the soup boils. The mixture need only boil a couple of minutes.

title: Chicken (Høn)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Salt
- Peppercorns
- Carrot
- Onion
- Parsley
- Butter
- Flour
- Egg

## Directions

1. When the chicken is cleaned, cut it up; remove the wings and legs first; separate the breast and back, and cut these into suitable pieces.
2. Place them over boiling water in boiling broth, which should cover them. Skim as often as foam rises, then add a tablespoon of salt, a handful of peppercorns, a carrot, an onion, a couple of parsley sprigs, and a bay leaf.
3. Simmer gently until the chicken is tender, approximately one hour, then remove it and keep it warm while the broth is strained.
4. The broth is strained and set back over the chicken to simmer; a piece of butter and an ounce of flour are stirred together over the fire; little by little, part of the broth is stirred in, adding a beaten egg and stirring until the sauce is smooth. Salt and pepper are added, and it is served on a warm dish.

title: Chicken Pudding (Chicken and Forcemeat Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Raw ground meat
- chicken
- sharp brown sauce

## Directions

1. Leg and wing are taken from the chicken, and from the back the lower piece is removed; the breast is divided into 4 pieces.
2. When they are well cooked and again cold, raw ground meat is placed in a baking dish, then a layer of the cooked chicken, and then again ground meat.
3. It is then fried in a roasting pan until the forcemeat is cooked; they are served with sharp brown sauce.
4. The chickens can also be filled inside with ground meat.

title: Chicken Salad (Høne Salat)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked chicken celeriac and celery
- 3 or 4 egg yolks
- mustard
- salt
- cold butter
- vinegar
- salad oil
- warm stock

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked chicken celeriac and celery into small pieces
2. The whites and yolks of the eggs are pressed separately and the oil is whipped slowly into the yolks
3. Then mix all ingredients in a bowl (stock excepted) and place over heat
4. It simmers until fairly thick
5. When cooled again, the stock is whipped in and poured over the salad one hour before serving

title: Chicken Shin and Macaroni (Chicken Shin with Macaroni)
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni
- beef or chicken stock
- chicken shin
- salt
- ground meat
- butter

## Directions

1. 1 pound of macaroni is cooked in as much beef bouillon that it barely covers them, until they are tender, but not too much cooked.
2. The chicken shin is cooked well and must not be too salty.
3. When this is cold, a mold is thickly covered with ground meat; in it are placed small slices of the lean part of the shin and the macaroni with their sauce, and then ground meat over.
4. You can either stir a little unbeaten butter into the macaroni while they are warm, or let a little of the fat remain on the shin pieces after cooking, as you prefer.
5. It must cook at least 3 hours.

title: Chocolate Eclairs
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- egg weight in sugar
- half the weight in flour
- one level tablespoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat 4 eggs and egg weight in sugar with half the weight in flour and one level tablespoon baking powder
2. Bake in biscuit forms; each form is buttered and one spoonful of dough is placed in each and they are baked at even, good heat
3. When they are almost cold, the round side is covered with caramel glaze

title: Chocolate Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 0.5 pound white sugar
- 6 tablespoons sifted flour
- 0.5 pound grated chocolate
- 1 teaspoon baking powder dissolved in a little rosewater

## Directions

1. Beat eggs for a quarter hour with white sugar, then with sifted flour, grated chocolate and baking powder dissolved in a little rosewater. Grease the pan with tallow and must be cold again before the batter is put on in small spots; not too thick, as they will run out. Bake light brown.

title: Chocolate Soup (Chocolate Gruel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound chocolate
- 2 quarts sweet milk
- egg yolks
- sugar
- vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Grate and cook 1 pound of chocolate in 2 quarts of sweet milk, stirring constantly until it boils.
2. After some time of boiling, pour it into the tureen where the yolks are whisked with sugar and a few drops of good vanilla extract; you must whisk into it until the soup is poured up.

title: Chocolate Soup (Chokoladsuppe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound chocolate
- 2 quarts milk
- egg yolks
- sugar
- a few drops vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Grate and cook 1 pound chocolate in 2 quarts milk, stirring regularly until it boils.
2. After some time of boiling, pour it into a tureen where 3 egg yolks have been whisked with plenty of white sugar and a few drops of good vanilla extract.
3. Whisk until the soup is completely poured in.
4. If desired, the egg whites can be whisked, cooked, and laid on top in the tureen.
5. Serve with soft bread or small rusks, calculating 1 pint of milk per person.

title: Chokoladefaøen (Chocolate Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1/2 pound soft sugar
- 6 tablespoons sifted flour
- 1/4 pound grated chocolate
- 1 tablespoon baking powder dissolved in a little rose water

## Directions

1. Beat the 4 eggs in a mixer for a quarter hour with 1/2 pound soft sugar
2. Then add 6 tablespoons sifted flour, 1/4 pound grated chocolate and 1 tablespoon baking powder dissolved in a little rose water
3. Butter the bottom of a baking pan and it must be cold again before the dough is placed on it in small strips; not too densely, as they will run out
4. In about a half hour they are usually done and are removed when they loosen from the pan

title: Chow Chow
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds cabbage
- 2 pounds cauliflower
- 1 pound cucumbers
- 6 celery knobs
- 6 ounces red peppers
- 1 quart small onions
- 2 quarts green tomatoes
- 2 gallons vinegar
- 1 pound mustard
- 4 pounds mustard seeds
- 1 jar French mustard
- 1 ounce cloves
- 2 ounces turmeric

## Directions

1. Chop the cabbage, cauliflower, cucumbers, celery, red peppers, onions and green tomatoes into small pieces
2. Boil each type of vegetable separately until tender, then drain
3. Put 2 gallons vinegar and the spices in a pot and bring to boil
4. Mix the vegetables and pour the hot vinegar mixture over them

title: Coffee Cream (Kaffe Creme)
body:
## Ingredients

- Strong extract of good coffee
- 1 quart cold cream
- 5 ounces sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 1 pint of coffee extract (or according to taste)

## Directions

1. Cook or draw a strong extract of good coffee
2. Beat 1 quart cold cream with 5 ounces sugar
3. Beat 10 egg yolks first alone, then with the cream
4. Add this in an enameled pot together with 1 pint of coffee extract (one can also add according to taste)
5. Stir constantly until it rises, and likewise when it is poured into a dish until it becomes cold, whereupon it is served in small cups with cakes

title: Cold Beef Salad (Salat af koldt Oksekød)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef
- shallot
- gherkins or anchovies

## Directions

1. Chop the beef, but not too fine and do not include any brown parts
2. Chop shallot and mix in together with finely chopped gherkins or anchovies
3. Prepare with vinegar and pepper

title: Conserve of Currants or Berries
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants or berries
- Loaf sugar
- Bitter almond or lemon zest

## Directions

1. For each pound of the largest and best berries, take 1 pound of loaf sugar, a little bitter almond or lemon zest, with which they are cooked and skimmed, until they are no longer sour, usually in a quarter.
2. They are poured off so that a cloth is spread over and the next day they are bound up. One can cook small pieces of apple together with the berries, which much improves them by removing the bitter taste.

title: Conserve of Melon
body:
## Ingredients

- Melon, not fully ripe
- Strong brine

## Directions

1. A melon that is not fully ripe is peeled, hollowed out and cut into finger-length pieces, about an inch wide and half as thick, rinsed, placed in a brine that is so strong that it can just make an egg float, and stays therein for 4 days. Then the pieces are taken up and placed for three days in...

title: Conserve of Melon or Cucumber
body:
## Ingredients

- Large cucumbers or small melons, still whole and green
- Loaf sugar
- Ginger

## Directions

1. Large cucumbers or whole small melons, both still quite green, are cut into large pieces, about an inch in width and half as thick, are rinsed, placed on a clean cloth to dry. The outer skin should remain on, where it is not on the cucumber too thick and hard.
2. Finely pounded loaf sugar is mixed with as much vinegar as it can dissolve; it is cooked until it is skimmed and clear, with 1 teaspoon of pounded ginger for each 2 pounds. Now the pieces get a good boil in the sugar, after which they are taken up, placed in a crock, and the sauce is poured lukewarm over. Should it not cover, then more must be cooked.
3. As the pieces tend to float up, they are pressed down with a spoon or a small round object, which is placed in the crock with something heavy on it. After 3 days they are taken up, to place the top pieces at the bottom. When they have stayed thus so that one can taste that they are thoroughly penetrated by the sugar, they are taken up and placed in a dry crock or a thick wood, and sprinkled between each day with dry loaf sugar.
4. This also becomes syrup, and both this and the first can be used to fill other cucumbers. The conserve pieces are now placed in layers in a press with paper above and below. When they are pressed, a string is drawn through them and they are allowed to hang in an airy place to dry, but not in the sun. Finally they are stored in a crock with soft white sugar between each layer. The fruit must not be allowed to dry too much.

title: Corn Meal Bread (Mais Brød)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts corn meal
- 3 pints warm water
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 2 large tablespoons homemade yeast or 2 cents compressed yeast
- wheat flour as needed
- 1 pint warm water

## Directions

1. Mix 2 quarts corn meal with 3 pints warm water
2. Add 1 tablespoon salt, 2 tablespoons sugar, and 2 large tablespoons homemade yeast or 2 cents compressed yeast
3. Let it rise 5 hours in a warm place
4. Add as much wheat flour as needed to make a dough that can be handled, and 1 pint warm water
5. Let it rise again for 1 hour, stirring occasionally
6. Knead it a little and divide it into bread pans
7. Let it stand a few minutes before placing in the oven
8. Bake in a hot oven for 1.5 hours
9. All bread made of corn meal should be eaten warm

title: Cream Barley Porridge (Hvede-Fladesup) (Wheat Flatbread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wheat bread, finely crushed
- Water
- Sugar
- A little salt
- Egg yolk (optional)

## Directions

1. Soak the finely crushed wheat bread in water until softened.
2. Stir in sugar and a little salt.
3. The soup can be enriched with an egg yolk if desired.

title: Cream Pie No. 1
body:
## Ingredients

- A scant pint fresh milk
- 2 small tablespoons corn starch
- 2 eggs
- A scant tablespoon sugar
- 0.5 cup butter
- 2 tablespoons lemon juice
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup white sugar
- 0.5 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 3 tablespoons cold water

## Directions

1. Cook a scant pint of fresh milk; in a little of the cold milk mix 2 small tablespoons corn starch; whip this together with 2 eggs.
2. When the milk has cooked, stir the mixture slowly in with a scant tablespoon sugar, 0.5 cup butter and 2 tablespoons lemon juice.
3. Then make a cake as follows: 3 eggs are stirred together with 1 cup white sugar, 0.5 cup milk, 1 teaspoon baking powder, 3 tablespoons cold water; it is divided into 2 ordinary pie pans and baked in a quick oven.
4. When the cakes are ready, take them out, and between both layers the cream is filled; if desired, it can again be glazed with egg white and sugar.
5. Four egg yolks are whipped stiff; then 1 cup sugar is stirred in and then 1 cup milk, which is heated together with 1 tablespoon baking powder; when it is stirred together, the whipped egg white is added.
6. It is divided into six ordinary pie pans and baked light brown. It will be enough for 2 pies.
7. A cream is made of 1 quart milk, 1 tablespoon corn starch, dissolved in a little cold milk, 3 eggs and a little vanilla.
8. When it is cold, it is divided in two parts and spread between two cake layers.

title: Currant Soup (Ribessuppe)
body:
## Ingredients

- currants
- water
- bread

## Directions

1. Made similarly, but many prefer to let the berries remain whole. In that case no bread is used.

title: Custard Omelette (Styret Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb butter
- 1/2 lb flour
- 1 pint milk
- 6 egg yolks
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Bake 1/2 lb butter and 1/2 lb flour until the spoon slides through it
2. While still over the fire, stir in 1 pint milk
3. When cooled, add 6 egg yolks and finally the beaten whites
4. Grease a frying pan with cold butter for the first layer
5. Divide the batter into 8 parts and bake as 3 thick pancakes
6. Between each layer butter compote is spread, and on the top powdered sugar is sprinkled

title: Druer (Grapes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound semi-ripe grapes
- half pound of the same sort of grapes
- 1 pound brown sugar

## Directions

1. Lay the semi-ripe grapes in a crock; crush half a pound of the same sort of grapes; strain the juice and mix with 1 pound brown sugar and pour over the whole berries, which stand covered for 2 days, then place the whole over fire and cook until the grapes have changed color, whereupon they are taken out and placed in the crock.
2. Cook the juice a little longer, skim and pour hot over the grapes, which are covered with a cloth and left for another 2 days.
3. Then place the whole over fire again; when it begins to cook, take out the grapes and place in the crock; cook the juice well, skim and pour hot over the grapes, which are covered with a cloth and tied the next day.
4. After they have stood for 8 days, the juice may have become thin; it is then cooked with an addition of 2 ounces sugar per pound and poured cold over the grapes, which are covered with paper dipped in brandy and tied.
5. They can also be preserved in the same way with powdered sugar, and are then good for cakes, sweet soup and sauces.

title: Enfoldig Kyllingesalte (Simple Boiled Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. This method is far preferable to any other.
2. The chicken is soaked in cold water for 12 hours; after that it is washed very well and the skin is scrubbed with a stiff brush.
3. Place it in the steamer, cover with a lid and set it over a stove with boiling water.
4. Cook approximately 22 minutes per pound.
5. The water is kept at a steady boil.
6. When the chicken is served warm, it is cut and a layer of bread crumbs is placed over it to prevent the juices from running out.

title: Eylet (Crab-Apple)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small, red crab apples
- Sugar
- Water
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Select small, red crab apples for this. The stems remain on, and they are placed in preserving liquid with enough distilled water to cover them.
2. Bring to boil and cook slowly until the skin cracks; then strain, cool and peel them; with a small knife pit them through the end where the blossom sits.
3. Weigh them; for one pound of fruit take 1.5 pounds sugar and a small cup of water.
4. Water and sugar are cooked and properly skimmed; then add the fruit and cook gently under cover until the apples are clear, red and tender.
5. Then take them out with a fork and spread on dishes to cool and become firm.
6. To the syrup add the juice of one lemon for every three pounds of fruit, and cook until it is thick and clear.
7. Fill the glasses or crocks three-quarters full with fruit, pour syrup over, and tie when cold.

title: Fersken Gele (Peach Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Peaches
- Peach kernels
- Lemon juice
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch the fruit, cut it into pieces and remove the stones. Cut one-third of the kernels into pieces and put them in the pot with the fruit. Now set it in boiling water, according to the instructions, stirring from time to time, until the fruit is well broken into pieces. Then strain it, and for each pint of peach juice take the juice of 1 lemon. Then measure it again and give 1 pound of sugar for each pint of juice. Heat the sugar as usual, and proceed according to the instructions. This jelly gives an exquisite flavor in cakes.

title: Fine Omelet of Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs (6, 8 or 10)
- Half an onion or veal stock
- Parsley
- Milk
- Cornstarch
- White pepper
- Salt
- Lemon juice
- Parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. A white sauce is prepared as follows: half an onion or veal stock is cut in strips and added together with some parsley in a quart of cold water, boiled and skimmed until the meat is cooked in pieces and half the soup has boiled away. Then it is carefully strained and returned to the pot with half a cup of milk. When it boils, thicken it with a tablespoon of cornstarch, stirred in a little cold milk, season with white pepper, salt, and the juice of half a lemon. Set aside in the oven and keep warm. Six, eight or ten eggs are boiled hard, the yolks carefully removed and the whites cut into thin strips. The yolks are now piled up in the middle of a deep dish, and the strips are arranged around it so it takes the form of a bird's nest; stir in the sauce and pour it carefully over the eggs. The sauce should go halfway up the nest. Garnish with parsley.

title: Fish Salad (Fisk Salat)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 1 pint stock
- mustard
- 3 to 4 spoonfuls vinegar
- white wine
- pepper
- salt
- lobster or anchovy essence
- cooked parsnip
- cooked small pasta

## Directions

1. Stir 4 egg yolks in 1 pint stock and cook as for cream
2. When cooled, stir in mustard, 3 to 4 spoonfuls vinegar, white wine, a little pepper and salt, and if desired, some lobster or anchovy essence
3. In this sauce, which must be smooth, add a cooked parsnip, shelled and in small finely broken pieces, and cooked macaroni
4. Mix well together and let it stand a couple of hours before using

title: Fish Salad No. 2 (Fisk Salat No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked fish
- cooked parsnip
- canned mushrooms
- stock
- vinegar
- soft roe and mustard blossom
- salt

## Directions

1. The fish can be of various kinds; when cooked, it is shelled from the skin and bones in flat pieces
2. Make a sauce from a pint of stock, which can preferably be sour, stirred with 2 spoonfuls split mushrooms together with their vinegar, a little soft roe and mustard blossom and salt
3. If the sauce is not sour enough, add a little vinegar
4. Instead of soft roe, salad oil can be used; instead of mushrooms, capers can be used

title: Fiskekompot (Fish Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds fish
- 4 quarts water
- Salt
- 1 large onion
- Carrots
- Parsley
- Butter
- 2 tablespoons lard
- Vinegar (if no stove available)

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into pieces and cook in 4 quarts water with salt, a large onion and carrots
2. When these are tender, remove them, drain and return to the soup when it is strained
3. Cook the fish completely, press it through a sieve and strain it
4. Pour a little hot water on it in the sieve to draw out the stock
5. Chop a small handful of carrots and parsley finely and mix with two tablespoons lard, stir in a scant half pint of broth, reduce somewhat with the soup which is again placed on the stove, add when it is hot, and stir until it boils; then place bread or fish roe on top
6. If you do not have a stove, you can stir a few tablespoons of vinegar into the lukewarm preparation

title: Fiskekräm-Suppe (Fish Cream Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 fillets
- 1 carrot
- 1 parsnip
- 1 onion
- Fish broth
- 1 tablespoon salt
- Half a head of endive salad leaves
- Several slices of cold cooked beets
- 1 quart milk
- 2 ounces butter
- Flour
- White pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Prepare fillets as for filet (see that recipe), cut a carrot, a parsnip and an onion; make a fish broth
2. Place these vegetables over the stove in 1.5 quarts cold water together with the fish and 1 tablespoon salt, and bring the whole to a boil
3. Then remove the fillet carefully, lay it between two kitchen towels to cool, and let the soup cook gently
4. Cut half a head of endive salad leaves into small pieces with an instrument, such as a knife or vegetable cutter, and briefly place in boiling, well salted water, then dry and lay in cold water until needed
5. Some slices of cold cooked beets are formed like the salad and handled in the same way; keep all of this aside until it is needed
6. Rub the rest of the fillet through a strainer with a pestle
7. Place 1 quart milk over the stove to boil; place 2 ounces butter over the stove at the side and stir in an equal amount of flour
8. Gradually add this mixture with the boiling milk and with a quart of the water in which the vegetables were cooked; season the soup with a little white pepper and some nutmeg, and if needed with a little more salt; stir in the ground fish and season the soup slowly with a whipped egg
9. When the soup is ready to serve, add fish, salad and beets, and serve at once so that the contrasting colors do not fade

title: Fluffisk (Fish Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour
- Fish
- Nutmeg or ginger
- Sugar
- Salt
- Egg yolk
- French bread
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Make a roux by melting a good piece of butter and stirring flour into it little by little until it has a suitable consistency
2. Add the fish and grated nutmeg or ginger
3. Cook it a little, then add a small piece of sugar and salt
4. Bind the fish with an egg yolk, which gives it a good flavor
5. Layer slices of French bread that have first been baked in butter on a pan, and pour the fish over
6. Alternatively, place the warm fluffisk in a deep pan, sprinkle a layer of soft bread made from a finger-spoonful over it, pour melted butter over it and set it in the oven where it should bake light brown
7. If the bread is to sink down into the fish, this should be fairly thick and is therefore better bound with two egg yolks
8. Alternatively, one can use boiled fish or cooked red fish, though the latter should be chopped fine and requires much butter
9. If one does not wish to use bread for the salted fish, small-cut cooked carrots and ginger can be added

title: French Beans, Method 1 (Finnedbønner)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- 1 ounce butter
- 1 tablespoon minced parsley
- 1 teaspoon minced onion
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Put the beans in boiling water and cook without a lid until they are tender. Drain the water from them and place them back over the fire with 1 ounce of butter, 1 tablespoon of minced parsley, 1 teaspoon of minced onion and a little salt and pepper. Serve warm.

title: Fresh Coconut Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh coconut (grated)
- 1.5 cups sour milk
- 3 egg yolks
- A little salt and sugar
- 1 tablespoon melted butter

## Directions

1. Grate fresh coconut; for 1 cup grated coconut use 1.5 cups sour milk, 3 egg yolks, a little salt and sugar, and 1 tablespoon melted butter.
2. The whole is whipped well for 5 to 6 minutes; the pan is lined as usual; it is filled and baked.
3. On top are placed the egg whites, whipped with sugar.

title: Fricassee of Poultry or Meat (Fricassee af tost Kylling)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat
- Parsley
- Onion
- Broth
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg yolks
- Port wine or sherry
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into fine slices and simmer gently for a quarter hour with a handful of chopped parsley, a peeled onion cut into pieces, 1 pint broth, a bit of butter, salt and pepper
2. Quickly stir together 2 egg yolks, a glass of Port wine or Sherry, and 1 tablespoon vinegar
3. Pour this mixture in slowly to thicken the sauce

title: Frikassé (Fricassée)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf, veal, beef, pork, or mutton
- butter
- wheat flour
- salt
- water
- bundled parsley and celery leaves
- whole celery and parsley root
- green peas (optional)
- egg yolks (optional)
- fresh parsley (optional)
- oysters or mushrooms (optional)

## Directions

1. The meat is cut into pieces the same way as for ragout
2. A knob of butter is melted and wheat flour is stirred in to make a thick sauce, thinned with water until it is a thick consistency
3. The meat is added with salt and thinned with water until it just covers it
4. A bundle of parsley and celery leaves is added but removed before it falls apart
5. Whole celery and parsley root are added and it cooks until the meat is tender, then these are removed and cut into sticks
6. The sauce is poured over the meat, which is kept warm on the fire
7. If green peas are added, no eggs or mushrooms are used, only a little fresh parsley added just before serving
8. For oysters: the beards are removed from the oysters and cooked in a little broth which is strained and added to the fricassée; the oysters should not be cooked with it but only stirred in the sauce after it is thickened with eggs
9. For asparagus: it is scraped, cut into short pieces and cooked tender in the sauce

title: Frugt Gele (Jellies)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fruit
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Put the fruit in a stone pot; set this in a pot with lukewarm water and set a cover over it. Let it cook with a closely fitting lid until the fruit is soft. If you do not have a fruit press, which is the easiest, press the fruit through a coarse sieve very hard, only putting in a few handfuls at a time. For each pint of juice take 1 pound of sugar. The juice alone is set over heat and while it heats the sugar is divided into several parts and added gradually. Place a pot in the oven, which should not be too hot; from time to time open the door and stir the sugar to prevent it from burning. When the juice has come to a boil let it cook for 20 minutes and skim if necessary. The sugar should be so hot that you cannot put your hand in it. Should it melt around the edges it is no harm, though it is better to avoid; the melted parts will only form into lumps in the syrup and can easily be removed. Once the sugar is added to the boiling syrup, while stirring very quickly; it will melt quickly. As soon as you are quite sure the sugar is melted, remove the spoon. Then let the juice just come to a boil and immediately take the pot from the fire. Roll the glasses in hot water and fill with boiling jelly. Tie it up the next day.

title: Fruit Cream (Frugt Creme)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint raw fruit juice, half raspberries
- Sugar
- 1 ounce gelatin
- 1 quart cream
- Beaten whites

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pint raw fruit juice, half raspberries, with sugar and 1 ounce gelatin until the gelatin is dissolved
2. Let it cool and add 1 quart cream that has been beaten to a foam
3. Continue to stir until it is cold
4. Then pour it into a mold that has first been rinsed, and from which it can be turned out again
5. It is eaten without sauce with small, dry cakes
6. It can be made in the same way from salted juice, which must then be warmed

title: Frukteddike med Blomster (Fruit Vinegar with Flowers)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cup apples
- 2 large tablespoons sugar
- 1 pinch each of cinnamon and cloves
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Bring the apples, sugar, and spices to a boil and simmer 5 minutes
2. Pour into a stone jar and cover with cloth
3. Let stand until completely cold
4. Fill small glasses or jars three-quarters full with berries, with or without stems
5. Pour the cold vinegar over them
6. Mix very well

title: Fruktpudding (Fruit Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1 tablespoon baking powder
- equal amount salt
- 4 ounces or 6 large tablespoons granulated sugar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 egg
- ½ teaspoon lemon extract
- ½ pint milk

## Directions

1. Mix flour together with baking powder and salt
2. Rub 4 ounces (or 6 tablespoons) granulated sugar and 2 tablespoons butter together well
3. When mixed but not creamed, add the flour little by little
4. Make a well in the center of the dough and add 1 egg, ½ teaspoon lemon extract, and ½ pint milk
5. Mix to a soft dough and pour into a mold that is well buttered and dusted with flour
6. Cook for approximately 1 hour
7. Test with a splinter of wood stuck down into it; if the splinter does not catch on it, it is done
8. Turn the pudding out of the mold and serve with cream sauce

title: Gele af Blaabær (Blueberry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Blueberries
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch the berries and cut them into pieces and for every five pounds of fruit take a cup of water. Put it in the pot and proceed in the usual manner according to the instructions.

title: Gele af Crab-Æbler (Crab-Apple Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Crab apples
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cut sour, unripe crab apples into pieces, but do not peel them. Neither do you remove the kernels. These last give a special fine aroma to the jelly. Put the fruit in a stone pot, which is set in a pot of hot water, and let it cook 8 to 9 hours. If the apples are very dry, take a cup of water for every 6 pounds of fruit. When it is cooked let it stand in the pot half the night. Next morning press out all the juice, and prepare the jelly in the usual manner, giving 1 pound of sugar for each pint of juice. Crab-apple jelly is considered one of the finest, both in appearance and in taste.

title: Gele af Hindbær og Ribs (Jelly of Raspberries and Red Currants)
body:
## Ingredients

- Red raspberries
- Red currants

## Directions

1. Make 2 parts red raspberries and one part red currants. This is extraordinarily good tasting, especially for cakes.

title: Gele af vilde Kirsebær og Ribs (Jelly of Wild Cherries and Red Currants)
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild cherries with stones
- Red currants

## Directions

1. Take 2 parts wild cherries, stones and all, and 1 part red currants. This jelly is not only good tasting, but is also a whole remedy in case of colds, etc.

title: Gooseberry Soup (Stikkelsbærssuppe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart gooseberries
- 2 quarts water
- 3 egg yolks
- white sugar
- whole cinnamon

## Directions

1. Can be made of both unripe and ripe gooseberries. It is made partly with eggs. Place 1 quart gooseberries over heat in 2 quarts water; they do not need to be hulled.
2. Meanwhile whisk 3 egg yolks with plenty of white sugar in the tureen.
3. When the berries are cooked, strain the soup and return to heat with a little whole cinnamon and sugar, as it is uncertain if the eggs will be sufficient to complement the berries' natural flavor.
4. When it has boiled some, whisk it into the eggs and serve with bread.

title: Graham Bread (Good against Dyspepsia)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart warm water
- Graham flour as needed
- 2 cents compressed yeast

## Directions

1. To 1 quart warm water, take as much Graham flour as is necessary to make a light dough
2. Add 2 cents compressed yeast
3. Let it rise in a warm place

title: Graham Muffins (Graham Flour Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups Graham flour
- 1 cup fine wheat flour
- 1 quart milk
- 2 cents worth of yeast
- 1 tablespoon butter or fat
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 2 tablespoons sugar

## Directions

1. Let it rise overnight
2. Bake in muffin tins for two minutes in a hot oven
3. Serve warm

title: Græskar (Pumpkins)
body:
## Ingredients

- pumpkins
- cold water
- brine
- boards
- weight

## Directions

1. Select ripe pumpkins that are not yellow. Peel, remove seeds, and cut into strips the thickness of apple peel and about a finger's length. Soak in cold water for 24 hours, then remove and place in brine for 24 hours. Then place between two boards with a light weight on top to extract the liquid, as pumpkins are more porous than cucumbers and absorb more brine. Dry well and preserve like pickled peppers.

title: Grønkål-Suppe (Kale Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart boiling water
- 1 quart good milk
- 1 teaspoon of fresh crackers
- White pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Blend 1 quart boiling water in the broth with 1 quart good milk; stir in 1 teaspoon of fresh crackers mixed with a little pepper and salt according to taste
2. When it boils add 1 quart good, fresh kale; you must stir so it does not burn; finally add a piece of good butter the size of an egg, and as soon as it boils up, the soup is ready and taken from the stove

title: Grønne Brier (Green Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas
- Butter
- Salt
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. If the peas are cooked with steam, when they are tender, place them on a plate and stir with a good spoonful of butter and a little salt.
2. If steam is not used, they can be cooked with sugar and very little water until tender; then stir in a good spoonful of butter and arrange them nicely.
3. They can also be cooked tender in a little more water and topped with a liaison of plenty of butter and a little flour together with parsley, salt and a little sugar.

title: Guldkage (Gold Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound clarified butter
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 1 pound fine flour
- 10 egg yolks
- beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Once 1 pound clarified butter is stirred to glow, it is stirred for a quarter of an hour with 1 pound powdered sugar and thereafter with 1 pound fine flour and 10 egg yolks, one at a time, finally the beaten whites.
2. It is poured into a greased form and baked at even heat, especially in the beginning, in 1.5 hours.

title: Gås eller Ånd med hvid Sauce (Goose or Duck with White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose or duck
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Bread or flour

## Directions

1. The meat becomes more flavorful when the sauce is cooked whole, but the sauce improves by being chopped up; it is also easier to serve.
2. To enjoy both advantages, one can half-cook the goose while it is whole, take it up when it is slightly cooled and then chop it up on a board with a sharp knife and a hammer.
3. Legs and wings are cut off first, breast and thigh are separated, and each of these parts is chopped into suitable pieces.
4. These become more tender than when the goose is chopped up raw; because then the bones often stick out of the meat when it shrinks during cooking, and it retains its strength better.
5. If one has time, then everything that belongs to goose soup can be taken with it for white goose, except the head; it is placed under the other on the board.
6. The soup must boil when the chopped meat is added to it again; when it is tender, it is placed in a warm dish with the sauce.

title: Handchokolade (Hand Chocolate)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound finest chocolate
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 pint cream
- 1 ounce white sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- Cold water

## Directions

1. Break the chocolate into small pieces and melt it in 1 tablespoon butter in a bowl over a water bath
2. Stir it into 1 pint of cream
3. Add 1 ounce white sugar and set in a high pot over the fire
4. As soon as it comes to a boil and has cooked for 5 minutes with stirring, place 2 egg yolks in a small cup of cold water and whisk them to a foam
5. Remove the chocolate and pour it little by little into the egg yolks while stirring
6. Set it again over the heat, where it is whisked with an egg whisk until it is about to boil
7. Then remove it, whisk for another 5 minutes and serve.

title: Handchokolade på Pariser Manier (Hand Chocolate, Parisian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fine chocolate
- 1 pint cream
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Grate the chocolate and dissolve it in 1 pint of cream
2. Place it in a high pot, set over gentle heat and cook
3. When it has cooked for 10 minutes, whisk it with an egg whisk until it froths strongly and pour it little by little into 2 egg yolks that are whisked throughout the entire work
4. Place it back in the pot and whisk continuously with the egg whisk
5. All the formed foam is transferred with a skimming spoon into pre-warmed cups, and this is continued until it stands piled up in the form of a dome over the cups
6. A tablespoon that is made glowing hot is held quite close over the foam until a kind of crust forms, after which the chocolate is served.
7. This chocolate froths even better when some sugar is stirred into the egg yolks, or also when foam birds are used, which are prepared in the following way: a sufficient number of egg whites are whisked to very stiff foam, and into it is stirred so much fine powdered sugar that the mass forms a stiff dough. This is spread on a baking sheet, and when it is about to dry, small birds are formed from it, of the size of a medium nut, which are then dried completely in the sun or by room heat. After the egg yolks in the above manner are whisked together with the chocolate, a foam bird is placed in the chocolate for each cup and whisked for a long time over the fire until all the chocolate mass is dissolved into almost a foam. One must observe the precaution that the chocolate is only warmed until nearly boiling; it must not boil at all.

title: Hard-Boiled Eggs with Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs (6, 8 or 10)
- Tongue or game bird
- Parsley
- Melted butter
- Mustard
- Sauce from game or veal stock
- Parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cream

## Directions

1. Hard-boiled eggs (cooked 10 minutes) are cut in half and the yolk is removed; it is finely mixed with a little chopped tongue or game bird, a little parsley, a little melted butter and quite a little mustard. This is stirred well together and with it the egg whites are filled, which are placed close together in a deep dish with sauce, the open ends facing up. Now some sauce from game stock or veal stock is prepared; this is brought just to boiling with half a tablespoon of chopped parsley, salt, pepper and finally three spoonfuls of cream. When it boils up, it is poured over the eggs, the dish is covered, it stands for five minutes, and is then sent to the table. As soon as the hard-boiled eggs are ready, place them in cold water, so the yolk does not become dark.

title: Hash (Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 parts cold roast or cooked salted beef (corned beef)
- 1 part mashed potatoes
- Pepper
- Salt
- Flour
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Mince the beef finely, take mashed potatoes, add a little pepper, salt, flour and melted butter.
2. Put this mixture in a roasting pan and stir it until it is heated through, but not browned.
3. The mixture can also be formed into flat cakes, dipped in beaten egg and fried in browned butter or lard.

title: Hel Laks (Whole Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon
- Water
- Salt
- Parsley
- Small shallots (optional)
- Celery (optional)
- Small roasted pepper (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean the salmon in 3 changes of water and rub lightly with a coarse cloth to remove the salt.
2. Afterwards, soak the salmon in lukewarm water.
3. Change the water in the morning with cold water, and the fish lies in it for 3 hours.
4. Then take it up, dry it and cut it into strips as in the previous method.
5. Place these in a pot with boiling water, which is poured beside a saucepan with stock prepared as for fresh salmon.
6. Beside these have the airtight glass jars with the lids lying ready, and when the salmon has cooked properly for five minutes, take the pieces up with a fork, and pass them quickly into the glass jars.
7. Fill them with the boiling stock from the other pot and seal tightly.
8. Two days after, the salmon can be taken into use and is almost as good as fresh pickled salmon.
9. It has the advantage that it can always be obtained and is relatively cheap.

title: Hindbær (Raspberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound good raspberries
- 1 pound white sugar

## Directions

1. Pick and mix 1 pound good raspberries with 1 pound white sugar
2. The next day put the whole thing on the stove and simmer 10-12 minutes with a couple of boilings, skimming
3. Then strain through a sieve so the juice can run off and cook and skim this for 8-10 minutes
4. When removed from the stove, add the berries to blend with the juice and pour into jars

title: Hindbær Kager (Raspberry Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- 1 pound patent flour

## Directions

1. Whip 12 eggs and 1 pound powdered sugar well together
2. Then stir in 1 pound patent flour
3. When this dough is well stirred together, press small cakes from it with a spoon, which you place on the buttered pan, sprinkle them with sugar and bake them quite light

title: Hindbær, Metode No. 2 (Raspberries, Method No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good raspberries
- White sugar, 2 pounds per quart of juice
- Thin cloth

## Directions

1. Set good raspberries aside for 24 hours, then strain the juice
2. Measure the juice and pour into a bag or sieve with a thin cloth laid in it, and let sit for another 24 hours
3. Then put it in the pan together with all the dregs and sediment that may have collected on top, plus 2 pounds white sugar for each quart of juice
4. Boil well but only for 5 minutes and skim well
5. The next day pour the juice into bottles and cork

title: Hummerolje-Suppe (Lobster Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 small lobsters
- 1 quart milk
- 1 pint water
- Butter
- 1 tablespoon lard
- Salt
- Red pepper

## Directions

1. Use 1 large lobster or 2 small ones; take all the meat from the shells and chop it finely; warm 1 quart milk together with 1 pint water; then add the lobster mixed with butter and 1 tablespoon lard mixed with flour, and salt and red pepper according to taste
2. Cook for 10 minutes

title: Hummerret (Lobster Dish)
body:
## Ingredients

- One medium lobster
- Salt
- Water
- Lobster meat
- Salad oil
- Bread
- Nutmeg
- Cayenne pepper
- Lemon juice
- Butter
- Egg yolk
- Parsley
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Choose a medium-sized lobster, not too large.
2. Put a handful of salt in a pot of boiling water and after having tied the claws together, fasten the lobster to the water.
3. Cook for half an hour to an hour, depending on its size.
4. When it is done, take it up and lay it with the back up on a sieve to dry.
5. When it is cold, scrape the body and tail and the claws.
6. Boil the lobster and pick out the meat, being careful that the shell remains intact.
7. Rub the shell with salad oil; into as much of the red roe as you have, add a little nutmeg, cayenne pepper, lemon juice, butter and a little egg yolk.
8. If it is lobster, rub the shell with egg yolk.
9. Put the meat back into the shells, cover lightly with soft bread and butter and bake brown in the oven.
10. Arrange with parsley and lemon slices around it.

title: Hvidkål (Pickled Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage hearts
- salt
- boiling water
- turmeric
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut cabbage hearts into 4 pieces, removing the outer green leaves. Boil in boiling water for 3 minutes. Remove, dry well, and cover with a thick layer of salt. Spread out in an oven to dry. Brush off the salt and place in cold vinegar in which enough turmeric has dissolved to color it well. Let stand for 2 weeks.

title: Hvost (Quinces)
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- Sugar (1 pound sugar per 1 pound quinces)
- Water (1 pint)
- Brandy for paper wrapping

## Directions

1. Put the quinces in boiling water and cook until they begin to soften.
2. Remove them, peel them, and cut them according to their size into 4 or 8 pieces.
3. Remove the core and all the stone around them, and weigh them.
4. For 1 pound of quinces take 1 pound of sugar, which is put on the stove with 1 pint of water; when the first scum is removed, put in the quinces, stir well, cook evenly for half an hour or longer, if needed, so that they can become perfectly tender and the syrup even.
5. Store them, cover a cloth over and the next day they are covered with paper, dipped in brandy, and tied.

title: Hvost-Marmelade (Quince Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- Quince peelings (1 pound)
- Water (1 pint)
- Quinces (1 pound)
- Sugar (1 pound sugar per 1 pound quinces)
- Currant juice (optional, for color)

## Directions

1. Dry the quinces, cut the peelings and weigh them.
2. For 1 pound of peelings take 1 pint of water, which they cook very gently for one hour, after which they are strained.
3. Slice the quinces in 4 to 8 pieces, remove the core and all the stone, weigh them.
4. For 1 pound of quinces take 1 pound of sugar, which is put in the water that has been strained from the peelings; when it is melted, put the quinces in and cook with the lid on, over a good and even fire, only not quickly, and with constant stirring, until they are tender.
5. This can take a couple of hours.
6. During this slow cooking, under a closed lid, the quinces often become red; if not, you can add currant juice.
7. When reduced to a soft pulp, stir in almost 1 pound of sugar for each pound of fruit; cook for another 10 minutes, stirring.
8. Remove from heat, and when cold, put into small jars or glasses with brandy paper over.

title: Hyldebær (Quinces)
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- For each pound of quinces: 1 pound sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Place in boiling water and cook until they begin to become soft.
2. Remove, peel, cut according to their size into 4 or 8 pieces, remove the seed cases and all the seedy matter around them, and weigh them.
3. For each pound of quinces take 1 pound sugar, which is set on the fire with 1 pint water.

title: Håndfager (Hand-Baked Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Milk
- Salt

## Directions

1. Heat butter on a griddle for the first pancakes
2. The griddle must be hot before you begin baking and butter is added to the griddle for the first pancakes, later not. The dough is held thin on the griddle and when the underside is baked enough, the pancake is turned.
