This is best done by loosening it around the edge with a knife, taking it at two spots at the edge and turning it quickly, after which it is baked finished on the other side
3. For the pancakes to become hard, they must be baked slowly over the griddle, not burning, as this gives too harsh heat, from which the pancakes become dark and not good; these should not be laid warm on top of one another. If you wish the pancakes soft, little butter is added to the griddle for each pancake, they are baked faster and laid warm on top of one another
4. If the dough becomes too thick during baking, it can be thinned with a little milk
5. The first pancakes are seldom whole, but when several are baked on the griddle, it goes better

title: Håndfager - Second Method (Hand-Baked Pancakes - Second Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread dough
- Milk
- Melted butter
- Egg
- Sugar
- Caraway seed
- Grated lemon peel

## Directions

1. A handful of bread dough is stirred, then stir in enough sour milk so the dough becomes a little thicker than it should be; add a little spoon of melted butter, 1 egg, a little handful of sugar and very little caraway seed and grated lemon peel

title: Håndfager i Fedt (Hand-Baked Pancakes Fried in Fat)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 4 ounces flour
- 1 quart milk
- 8 tablespoons sugar
- Egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir together 8 egg yolks, 4 ounces flour and 1 quart milk well with 8 tablespoons sugar, whisk the egg whites and fold them into the dough
2. The dough is held quite thin on the griddle, and when the pancake is baked on one side, it is turned together without being baked on the other, and laid straight on the table
3. Jam can be laid inside. For this 2 pounds is needed

title: Håndfager med Kardamomme (Hand-Baked Pancakes with Cardamom)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons white sugar
- 12 tablespoons rum
- 6 small tablespoons melted butter
- Little grated lemon peel
- Ground cardamom
- 1½ pints milk or thin gruel
- Beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Whisk together 8 egg yolks well with 3 tablespoons white sugar
2. Stir in 12 tablespoons rum, 6 small tablespoons melted butter, a little grated lemon peel, ground cardamom, 1½ pints milk or thin gruel and fold in the beaten egg whites

title: Hønesuppe (Chicken Soup) [Especially Recommended]
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken of 2 pounds
- 2 quarts cold water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Kill and pluck the chicken, remove all fat from the interior and clean it carefully, so that you avoid breaking the gall and intestines
2. Cut up the meat and break the bones
3. Place it in a cooking pot with 2 quarts of cold water, bring slowly to a boil and skim until it is clear
4. Cover with a lid and simmer gently until the chicken has become a cohesive mass, namely from 2 to 4 hours
5. Strain it through a fine cloth until it is clear, and set it back over the heat to simmer gently until it has become a complete jelly. This is best recognized by placing a spoonful of the jelly on a saucer; if it, after being cooled, is firm, then it is ready
6. It can be seasoned lightly, depending on what the doctor allows, and kept in cups; serve cold or slightly warmed

title: Hønsekjødsuppe (Chicken Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- water
- beef or veal broth
- half meatballs
- bread or flour dumplings
- parsley
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make it like other beef soup; however, take only water for the portion of soup you desire, if the hens are young; if they are old and tough, you must take nearly double of what you wish to keep
2. Cut up the hens so that legs and wings are removed; next the thighs are separated from the breast; the breast is chopped into 2-3 pieces; the breastbone is removed, and each half is divided in two
3. Include legs, feet and inner organs
4. Use only half meatballs on chicken soup, and bread or flour dumplings, parsley and a little nutmeg
5. Serve with toasted bread

title: Jordbær (Strawberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- A couple tablespoons brandy
- 1 pound strawberries
- 1 pound fresh, not too ripe strawberries
- Sugar, 1 pound

## Directions

1. To make excellent preserved strawberries, cook 1 pound sugar to syrup
2. Add a couple tablespoons brandy, then 1 pound strawberries and bring to boil
3. Pour out and let sit 2-4 days
4. Then take 1 pound fresh, not too ripe strawberries
5. Pour the juice from the first batch over these and let sit a few days
6. Carefully remove the strawberries to a sieve, strain the juice so the seeds come out, and cook with 1 pound more sugar
7. This is then poured cold over the berries
8. Strawberries should be picked the same day they are preserved, otherwise they taste bitter when preserved

title: Jordbær Marmelade (Strawberry Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound sugar per pound fruit
- 1 pint currant juice for 4 pounds strawberries

## Directions

1. For each pound of fruit use 0.5 pound sugar
2. For 4 pounds strawberries take 1 pint currant juice and cook together with the strawberries for half an hour, stirring the whole time
3. Remove nearly all the juice from the berries, add the sugar, and let boil rapidly for 20 minutes, skimming carefully the whole time
4. Put in small glasses with brandy paper over
5. The currant juice can be omitted, but makes the taste much finer

title: Kalvebrysti (Braised Calf Breast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds calf breast
- vegetables saved from broth
- 1 small onion
- 2 thin slices of meat
- salt
- pepper
- brown sauce

## Directions

1. The breast is laid in a braising pan on a bed of vegetables and a small onion
2. 2 thin slices of meat are laid on top together with a little salt and pepper
3. It is baked in a fairly hot oven for approximately one hour until completely tender
4. It is served with brown sauce, which is made by rubbing the sauce in the braising pan together with the vegetables through a sieve, blended with the boiling water and seasoned to taste

title: Kalveragout (Calf Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds calf meat
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 small diced onion
- 2 large tablespoons wheat flour
- curry powder to taste
- salt
- boiling water

## Directions

1. The meat is chopped into small pieces and blanched in several changes of water
2. 2 tablespoons butter is placed over the fire in a pot; when melted, not browned, a small diced onion is added and cooked for a couple of minutes
3. Then 2 large tablespoons of wheat flour is stirred in, along with curry powder to taste and salt (use curry powder sparingly, about half a teaspoon, as more can be added later)
4. It is cooked together and gradually thinned with boiling water until it becomes a thick sauce
5. The meat is added in, and it should gradually be thinned with a bit more water, which should always be boiling
6. The sauce should be fairly thick but it cooks down
7. Cook covered if possible, but skim properly
8. It is done when the meat is tender; from 2 pounds of meat it can cook for about an hour
9. If the sauce becomes lumpy, remove the meat first and strain the sauce over it

title: Kamajak Stykker (Kamajak Pieces)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 2 eggs
- 2 cups sour milk
- 1 teaspoonful baking soda dissolved in warm water
- Flour

## Directions

1. Mix together 2 cups sugar, 1 cup butter, 2 eggs, 2 cups sour milk, 1 teaspoonful baking soda dissolved in warm water
2. Add flour to make the dough suitable thickness, work out, cut and fry like the previous ones

title: Kanesteg (Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- Heart
- Liver
- Fat meat, finely chopped
- Butter
- Water or broth
- Flour
- Lemon
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. Have the butcher skin the hare, then clean it.
2. Cook the heart and liver and chop them finely, then mix with a little finely chopped fat meat.
3. Make a forcemeat as for sausages and stuff the chopped liver and heart into it.
4. Stuff the hare with this forcemeat and sew it shut.
5. Place the hare in a pan in the usual manner with a little butter and broth, with which it is braised.
6. It usually takes about an hour to cook.
7. A few minutes before removing it from heat, dust it with flour and then lay butter on it again.
8. Add a small glass of wine to the sauce and a little lemon juice.
9. Arrange lemon slices on the platter around the hare.
10. Cut off the head before sending it to the table.
11. Serve with lingonberry preserve.

title: Kantucci af Rækkehøn (Stuffed Duck Shoulder)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds Shoulder
- Eggs
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Root vegetables
- Parsnip root vegetables
- Bone broth

## Directions

1. Take a 4 pound shoulder that should not be crushed at the butcher, wash it and lay it on the board with the inner part facing up.
2. Cut out the shoulder blood and boil it to make it completely clean.
3. Then cut out with a sharp, pointed knife the meat along the lower edge of the bone and the hallobone from the other, being careful to remove the hallobone between the first and second rib and be careful not to break the meat or cut through the bone.
4. Shape the end of the bone so it resembles a duck leg.
5. Fill the shoulder with one or another forcemeat or stuffing, though the most commonly used is prepared as follows: boil eggs, scrape them and cook them in three different ways; dissolve breadcrumbs in lukewarm water and strain in a clean cloth.
6. When the eggs are soft, which should take about half an hour, let them cool and mix together with the breadcrumbs, with a bit of butter, two egg yolks and a little salt and pepper.
7. After it is stuffed in the meat, bind it together so it resembles a duck.
8. Place it in a roasting pan on some slices of vegetables and roast in a hot oven for approximately 20 minutes per pound.
9. Meanwhile prepare root vegetables and parsnip root vegetables by cutting them into small pieces, cook them tender in broth and salted water, and place them in cold water to preserve their color.
10. When the roast is done, take it up on a warm platter and keep it warm without drying while the sauce is prepared.
11. The sauce is prepared from browned butter with a little flour, upon which the broth from the roasting pan is poured from the vegetables into the sauce, and finally a spoonful of raw egg yolk and a little powdered sugar are stirred in.
12. Warm the vegetables in the sauce and arrange them around the meat.
13. The shoulder blade should be scraped after being cooked and have the broad end attached so it resembles feathers, then placed in the sauce beside the roast, which is then served.

title: Kartoffel Pudding med Fiskekød (Potato and Fish Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds potatoes
- 2 pounds fish
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes, peel and rub with a light hand so there are no lumps
2. Bake in ½ pound butter
3. When baked, stir in 15 eggs and plenty of milk, pepper and salt
4. Cook for 3 hours and serve with strong brown sauce

title: Kartoffelkake (Potato Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound peeled raw potatoes
- 10 egg yolks
- 1.5 cups powdered sugar
- 1 tablespoon baking powder
- 5 cents' worth of sweet almonds
- 1 cent worth bitter almonds
- juice of 2 lemons and the clean peel of 1 lemon
- 10 beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. The potatoes are cooked and peeled the day before they shall be grated, which must be done with a light hand, but to guard against lumps one can only use what falls behind the grater.
2. To 1 pound freshly grated raw potatoes take 10 egg yolks, which are beaten well with 1.5 cups powdered sugar, 1 tablespoon baking powder, mixed well in the potatoes, for 5 cents' worth of sweet almonds and for 1 cent worth of bitter almonds, the latter ground very finely, juice of 2 and the clean peel of 1 lemon.
3. When this, together with the potatoes is stirred and beaten well in a crock, the 10 beaten whites are stirred gently therein.
4. The cake is baked straight away at even heat, especially until it has risen, then at stronger, so that it can be finished in an hour's time.

title: Kartoffelkroketter (Potato Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Butter
- Salt
- White pepper
- Nutmeg
- Egg yolks
- Egg white

## Directions

1. Press cooked potatoes through a coarse sieve.
2. Mix with a piece of butter, salt, a very little white pepper, a little grated nutmeg and two egg yolks.
3. Spread the mixture out on a board and cut with a knife into small cakes two inches long and one inch wide.
4. Place on a buttered board, brush with egg white and bake in the oven until golden brown.
5. The oven should not be very hot.

title: Kartoffelpuré (Mashed Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Broth or milk
- Butter
- Salt
- White pepper
- Nutmeg
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Peel potatoes raw, cut into pieces and place on heat with enough broth or milk to cover the potatoes.
2. Cook until completely softened, drain and press through a sieve.
3. Cook the mixture again with butter, salt, and if too thick, with more broth or milk.
4. Cooked potatoes can be riced and need not be pressed through a sieve.
5. Water may be used instead of broth, but then more butter must be used.

title: Kartoffelsuppe (Potato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- carrots
- parsnips
- turnip
- salt
- marjoram or sage
- soup from salt meat or fish
- fresh fish

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes raw, cut them into pieces and set them on the fire with enough cold water to cover them
2. When they have cooked somewhat, add carrots and parsnips and a good turnip; remove this when it is tender and press it well
3. Cook some whole small onions with it along with salt and a little marjoram or sage
4. You can add a little less water later and eventually season the soup with soup from salt meat or fish
5. Fresh fish can also be cut into small cubes, cooked with the potatoes and served in the soup

title: Kirsebær (Cherries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Morello or red cherries with stems
- Sweet cherries may be used
- Sugar, 1 pound to 1.5 pounds per pound of fruit

## Directions

1. Remove the stones, taking care to collect all the juice
2. Weigh the fruit and take 1 pound to 1.5 pounds sugar
3. Layer gently in the pan
4. Pour the juice over and simmer until the juice begins to thicken

title: Kirsebærmarmelade (Cherry Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- Best quality dark ripe cherries
- Sugar, 3 ounces per pound of berries
- Cinnamon
- Vinegar, if to be used for table

## Directions

1. Remove stems and stones using a cannelle spoon, rinse clean in several changes of warm water
2. Dry in cloths and spread in air or in an oven over coals until soft
3. Then crush gently so the kernels do not break, and remove the kernels
4. Weigh the berries and take 3 ounces sugar per pound
5. Heat sugar and a little of the juice from the berries, and if for table use, a little vinegar
6. When sugar is melted, add the berries and simmer gently until no longer tart, but not so long they become mushy
7. Pour onto a sieve
8. Heat the juice with cinnamon and simmer another quarter hour until even, then add the berries with the crushed kernels and a little orange peel to blend with the juice, but pour into jars and let cool the next day before sealing
9. Can use 0.5 pound sugar per pound berries, but must cook longer to keep and yields less juice

title: Kogt Frukt (Cooked Fruit)
body:
## Ingredients

- Raspberries, currants, pears, and all kinds of fruit
- Water
- Sugar
- Corn starch

## Directions

1. In summer, cook in very little water with sugar and an imperceptible seasoning of corn starch.
2. Pears and apples are peeled and cut in pieces.
3. Cherry stones are removed.
4. For cherries, add a few whole cloves and no seasoning is used.

title: Kokosmakroner (Coconut Macaroons)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons syrup
- 2 tablespoons rubber sugar
- 1 pound butter
- 2 ounces prepared potash dissolved in a gill of rose water
- 6 pounds ice beaten quickly
- 1 ounce grated nutmeg
- 1/2 ounce ground cinnamon
- 4 ounces finely chopped, split apple pits
- 1/2 pound ground almonds

## Directions

1. Heat 4 tablespoons syrup, 2 tablespoons rubber sugar, and 1 pound butter (which is clarified) over the fire
2. When it boils, add 2 ounces prepared potash dissolved in a gill of rose water
3. When it stops foaming, lift the pot from the fire but let it stand on the back of the stove until it no longer foams; then pour it into the pan one uses for bread
4. Beat 6 pounds ice by handfuls and quickly, likewise with 1 ounce grated nutmeg, 1/2 ounce ground cinnamon, 4 ounces finely chopped, split apple pits, and 1/2 pound ground almonds (the last two parts can be omitted)
5. Knead until it releases the hands and set it in a warm place where it can stand for several days
6. From this the dough becomes stiff; when you want to bake, pieces of dough are cut, placed on a baking sheet, spread out like small cakes, and shaped, half with a pastry bag in whatever forms you like, topped with chopped almonds and baked in a hot oven for half an hour

title: Kolaboller (Cardamom Buns)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart cold malt
- A little cup sugar
- ½ cup clarified butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 8 cardamom seeds, finely crushed
- A quart tablespoonful of citron oil
- Flour
- 3 tablespoons baking powder
- Beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix together 1 quart cold malt, a little cup sugar, ½ cup clarified butter, 4 egg yolks, 8 finely crushed cardamom seeds, a quart tablespoonful of citron oil with as much flour as makes the dough a suitable thickness
2. Stir together well and bake like the previous. Cardamom buns baked in goose or duck fat are excellent

title: Kolaboller - Variation (Cardamom Buns - Variation)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- ½ pound powdered sugar
- 12 eggs
- Little nutmeg
- Flour

## Directions

1. Mix together 1 pound butter, ½ pound powdered sugar, 12 eggs, a little nutmeg, and flour to make the dough suitable consistency
2. Cut into various mold shapes with the cookie cutter and fry like the smaller buns

title: Kødpølse (Meat Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lb. lean beef
- 6 lb. round steak
- 1 lb. salt
- a small amount (in ounce) of pepper
- a small amount (in ounce) of allspice
- a small amount (in ounce) of ginger
- 3 to 4 lb. fatty beef
- pig or pork intestines

## Directions

1. Cut and finely chop the meat, or grind it using a meat grinder
2. The meat should be fine enough to form a stiff mass
3. Work the meat alone for half an hour, then work it with 1 lb. salt, a small amount of pepper, a small amount of allspice, a small amount of ginger, the ginger fairly coarse, and mix in 3 to 4 lb. fatty beef cut into small cubes
4. The mixture should be fairly hard and must be worked until it forms bubbles
5. Prepare the intestines by emptying them, cutting them to suitable lengths, and placing them in saltwater for two days
6. Turn them over and place in saltwater again for one day
7. Scrape them, rinse in mild soda water, dry them, tie one end together and blow air in from the other
8. Fill using a sausage funnel, squeezing the intestine and only filling a small piece at a time
9. Once the sausages are well stuffed, rub them with salt and let them rest for 24 hours
10. Then wipe the mixture away

title: Lemon Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound powdered sugar
- 0.5 pound flour
- 4 eggs separated
- juice and half the zest of 1 lemon
- plenty of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Bake on a greased sheet in long narrow cakes. It is advisable to test one and should it run out, beat the batter still firmer and add just a bit of flour, but the oven must be quite hot and the cakes should be golden brown. It tastes good afterward to dip them in chocolate glaze or caramel.

title: Lemon Cream Sauce (Citron Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- cream
- salt
- butter
- flour
- bouillon
- lemon

## Directions

1. One pint of cream is boiled very gently with a little salt, kept warm and the juice of half a lemon. When it has boiled a little, a roux is made of two to eight ounces of butter (depending on how thick the cream is) mixed with a tablespoon of flour. This is stirred slowly until it becomes thick and then finished with bouillon until the sauce has acquired the right consistency. When it is dished up, the juice of half a lemon is pressed into it. Good milk can be used instead of cream but then more butter is used. Instead of lemon juice and juice, there can be so much strong egg yolk or good soda cooked into the sauce that it gets a reddish color.

title: Lemon Omelette No. 2 (Citron Omelet No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg yolks
- egg whites
- 1 cup powdered sugar
- juice of 1 large or 2 small lemons
- zest of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Beat 5 egg yolks white with 1 cup powdered sugar
2. Then beat with juice of 1 large or 2 small lemons and zest of 1 lemon
3. Then add the beaten egg whites, which must be stirred carefully around to blend well with the batter
4. Bake for 1.5 hours

title: Lemon Pie No. 1
body:
## Ingredients

- pie dough
- zest of 2 lemons
- 1 grated apple
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cup sugar
- a small piece of melted butter
- 2 tablespoons corn starch
- juice of 2 lemons
- a little warm water

## Directions

1. Line the pan with ordinary pie dough.
2. Rub together the zest of 2 lemons with a grated apple; whisk 2 egg yolks with 1 cup sugar, a small piece of melted butter, 2 tablespoons corn starch and the juice of 2 lemons; stir in a little warm water, and when it is all well mixed, pour it over the dough.
3. Then handle it exactly as Egg Pie: bake, spread with butter and place in the oven again.

title: Lemon Pie No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- pie dough
- zest and juice of 1 lemon
- 1 cup sugar
- yolks of 3 eggs
- 2 cups milk
- 1 tablespoon corn starch

## Directions

1. Blend the zest and juice of one lemon with 1 cup sugar and the yolks of 3 eggs; stir well.
2. Mix 1 tablespoon corn starch in 2 cups milk; place over heat and bring just to a boil; stir into the other ingredients and pour into the pie pan, which is lined with good dough.
3. When baked, spread the whipped egg whites on top, whisked with sugar.

title: Lemon Sauce (Citronsauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon corn starch
- Juice of 4 lemons
- 1 pint wine and white, whipped cream according to taste

## Directions

1. Beat 8 egg yolks well; add 1 tablespoon corn starch, juice of 4 lemons, 1 pint wine and white, whipped cream according to taste
2. Place this on heat under constant stirring until it begins to boil and becomes stiff
3. Then stir in 4 whites that are beaten to a foam, whereupon it is removed from heat and stirred constantly until it becomes cold

title: Lobster Roe Salad (Hummerrogn Salat)
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster green
- lobster roe
- stock or fine salad oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- watercress
- salad leaves

## Directions

1. Rub the lobster green in a little stock or fine salad oil; add the juice that flows from the lobster when opened, which must be carefully collected, and also a little vinegar and pepper
2. Cut the roe itself into small pieces and place between small tender leaves of salad lettuce in the sauce
3. Garnish with watercress, some of the finest salad leaves, and the red roe inside, cut in figures and placed among the greens

title: Lobster Salad (Hummerselat)
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster
- fresh green salad
- hard-boiled egg yolks
- melted butter
- mustard
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop the lobster finely
2. Cut three heads of fresh green salad finely; layer it in a fairly deep bowl
3. Rub out four hard-boiled egg yolks, stir in three spoonfuls melted butter, one teaspoon mustard, a little cayenne pepper and salt, two spoonfuls sugar and two cups vinegar
4. Place over heat and when warm, pour over the salad

title: Løg-Suppe (Onion Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 saucepan full of peeled onions
- 6 spoonfuls of flour
- butter
- soup

## Directions

1. Brown the peeled onions in butter
2. Stir in 6 spoonfuls of flour
3. Add the soup gradually
4. Let it simmer for some time
5. Skim off the foam
6. Bring to a boil again
7. Serve with toasted bread

title: Macaroni
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. It is broken into fairly long pieces and should be cooked in bouillon, which does not need to be very strong. There should not be more soup on than one can imagine suitable for it to cook into. When you take one of them up with the side of a fork and it bends easily in the middle, then they are done. Thereafter a little cold butter is stirred into them until the butter is melted. They must be arranged immediately and served with a sharp oil and melted butter.

title: Macaroni med Smør (Macaroni with Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni
- Salted water
- Butter
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Boil the macaroni in salted water and drain the water from them.
2. Butter a deep dish with butter, and layer the macaroni with a layer of pepper and butter.
3. The top layer should be macaroni and then small pieces of butter are laid on it.
4. Bake until it is brown.

title: Macaroni med Østers (Macaroni with Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni
- Salt
- Water
- Butter
- Oysters

## Directions

1. Cook macaroni in salted water and drain the water from it
2. Butter a deep pan, and layer macaroni with a layer of raw oysters and oysters
3. The top layer should be macaroni and over it lay small pieces of butter
4. Bake until it is brown

title: Macaroni Neapolitan Style
body:
## Ingredients

- Macaroni
- Water or light bouillon
- Minced cooked meat (veal or pork)
- Cooked ham
- Parmesan cheese
- Light bouillon
- Tomatoes

## Directions

1. Let macaroni cook in water or light bouillon until they are almost completely cooked. Then drain the water so only a little remains, with which they can move and slide. In another saucepan cook finely-cut and boned meat and ham in light bouillon until well cooked. Then remove it from the sauce, and thereafter layer everything in a deep dish, so that you first lay a layer of macaroni, and sprinkle them with grated Parmesan cheese, then a light layer of the meat, then macaroni again with cheese, and then meat and ham, and continue this way as long as you have the ingredients; but the last must be cheese. Then set the dish in a warm oven, approximately 10 minutes, until it is well warmed and has taken on a golden-brown color on top. Serve hot.

title: Macatoni Budding (Macaroni Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- a cupful of macaroni
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 8 egg yolks
- 1/2 pound sugar
- juice and grated peel of half a lemon
- beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Break the macaroni into thumb-length pieces and cook tender in a quart of milk
2. While hot, stir in 2 tablespoons butter, 8 egg yolks beaten with 1/2 pound sugar, juice and grated peel of half a lemon, and lastly the beaten egg whites
3. Pour into a buttered mold about half an hour, or until nicely brown

title: Madeira Sauce for Stew (Madeira Sauce til Steo)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- beef broth
- meat drippings
- chopped onion
- chopped anchovies
- mustard
- sauce of stewed oysters
- pepper

## Directions

1. Brown a piece of butter in a roasting pan, but not too much. Add two tablespoons of flour and rub it out with the back of a spoon. Be careful to move it from one place to another to prevent it from burning on the stove. The butter must not burn too strongly during the work. When it has acquired a nice brown color, gradually add beef broth and drippings from the roast. When this has been mixed a few times but is still too thick, add stock until it becomes the right consistency. Add chopped onion, chopped anchovies, a little undiluted mustard, sauce of stewed oysters as desired and pepper. Boil everything well together and strain it. Put the sauce back in the pan to warm it and add a little soda. If the roasting pan is only iron and not well lined with steel, do not keep the wine in the pan, but when the sauce has been made with broth, it is put into a small tinned copper pan or glazed earthenware and finished there. This sauce is good for beef roast and can also be used for goose. For goose, duck and pork more mustard is used.

title: Mais med Smør (Corn with Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Corn
- Milk
- Butter
- Salt

## Directions

1. Remove the kernels and cook until tender.
2. Coat in hot milk and prepare a sauce of milk and butter; add a little salt.

title: Maismelpap (Cornmeal Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Milk
- Cornmeal
- Salt

## Directions

1. In the preparation of this very nourishing and economical dish, one usually lets half water and half milk boil, then stirs in as much cornmeal as it gets a proper consistency, lets it boil a short time with stirring, and adds salt at serving.

title: Malt Pie No. 1
body:
## Ingredients

- A scant pint fresh malt
- Corn starch
- Egg yolks
- A little vanilla and salt
- Regular dough

## Directions

1. Cook a scant pint of malt; dissolve 1 tablespoon corn starch, 3 egg yolks, a little vanilla and salt.
2. Line the pan with regular dough, the filling is added, and it is put in the oven and baked at good heat.
3. Egg whites are whipped with sugar and placed on top when it is done, after which it is put back in the oven for a moment.

title: Malti Rullet (Rolled Malt Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- Dough made like biscuits
- Melted fat

## Directions

1. Make dough like for biscuits and cut out on the same way, except the strips are twice as long
2. Smear the top of each strip with melted fat
3. Bake in a hot oven

title: Mandel Kager (Almond Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 pounds flour
- 1/2 pound melted butter
- 1/2 pound rubber sugar
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons baking powder dissolved in a little rose water

## Directions

1. Mix 1 1/2 pounds flour, 1/2 pound melted butter, 1/2 pound rubber sugar, 2 eggs and 2 tablespoons baking powder dissolved in a little rose water well
2. Beat and spread out and press off with a cup
3. They are covered with egg yolk, sprinkled with sugar and topped with chopped almonds

title: Mandelkrandse (Almond Wreaths)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sweet and 2 ounces bitter almonds
- 1.5 pounds dry powdered sugar
- clean peel of lemon
- 6 egg whites beaten to stiff foam

## Directions

1. 1 pound sweet and 2 ounces bitter almonds are shelled and blanched.
2. Then they are stirred with 1.5 pounds dry powdered sugar, clean peel of lemon and 6 egg whites, first beaten to stiff foam.
3. One now forms wreaths of the dough, one constantly smaller than the other, so they can be laid one on top of the other when they are baked, and form a whole cake.
4. They are baked lightly at even heat.
5. If one only bakes small wreaths, there result approximately 40 of these.

title: Marmelad af Stikkelsbær (Gooseberry Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe gooseberries
- Sand
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Pick the ripe gooseberries and cook them in a pot for one hour with just enough sand in the bottom of the pot so they cannot burn on the bottom. Then take 1 pound of sugar to each pound of berries, and now cook the berries again for one hour with the sugar. Stir and grind in a colander the whole time.

title: Marmelade af Stikkelsbær (Marmalade of Gooseberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe gooseberries
- Water

## Directions

1. Peel the ripe gooseberries and cook them in an hour with just enough water in the bottom of the pot so they do not burn

title: Maître d'Hôtel Butter (Flavored Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- One ounce butter
- One tablespoon minced parsley
- One tablespoon lemon juice
- One small teaspoon pepper

## Directions

1. Blend together cold and spread thus over what you wish to use it for.
2. It is excellent for any kind of roasted fish.

title: Meat Balls in Sauce (Meat Balls with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef or fish stock
- water
- ground meat
- butter
- onion
- pepper
- nutmeg
- salt
- flour
- stock
- wine
- lemon juice
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Stock and water, equal amounts of each kind, are put on the fire, in which meat or fish balls are cooked; when they are cooked, they are taken up and the stock is strained.
2. A large spoonful of butter is put on the fire, in which a little chopped onion, a little ground pepper, nutmeg, salt and a large spoonful of flour are stirred, which is mixed with the stock until it has a suitable thickness, then the balls are added and cooked a little.
3. In the same way the balls can be prepared with oysters, mussels, mushrooms, capers, celery, or whatever you want to use.
4. The onions can be omitted and you can add a little wine and lemon juice, and enrich the sauce with a couple of egg yolks.
5. If you don't have stock, you can cook the balls in water.

title: Meat Cakes (Fried Meat Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ground meat
- butter
- greens
- hot water

## Directions

1. The mixture is made as firm as for meatballs, and from it small round patties are fried in brown butter.
2. They are placed on a platter with a sauce, and the spoon is dipped in hot water for each time in order for the sauce to cover them.
3. They are served with greens or with sharp sauce and potatoes.

title: Mel Pudding (Flour Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces flour
- 4 ounces butter
- 3 gills milk
- 8 egg yolks
- handful of sugar
- little cardamom
- one ounce salt

## Directions

1. Bake flour well with 4 ounces butter
2. Cook 3 gills milk and pour boiling into it gradually
3. When baked, take it up and let it cool
4. Stir together 8 egg yolks, handful of sugar, little cardamom and one ounce salt very well
5. Add the beaten whites gradually
6. Cook for 2 hours and serve with fruit or wine sauce
7. This cannot be baked

title: Midfiske-Pudding med Poteim (Whitefish Pudding with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fish, cooked
- chopped parsley
- 3 tablespoons cooked potatoes
- 2 tablespoons butter
- a little finely chopped mace flowers
- 3 whole eggs
- a pinch of grated lemon peel
- a few tablespoons chopped anchovies

## Directions

1. The fish and parsley are mixed well with 3 whole eggs, a pinch of grated lemon peel, and a few tablespoons of chopped anchovies
2. The mixture is placed in a buttered mold and set directly in the oven
3. Served with stirred or melted butter
4. For 5 persons

title: Minced Beef Steak (Minced Beef Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- Round Beef
- egg yolk
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- browned butter
- potatoes

## Directions

1. The meat is scraped from the sinews, which is done so that everything is included and only the clear sinews remain.
2. The meat is then formed with the blade of a knife into round, flat patties; salt and pepper are sprinkled on and they are fried in abundant browned butter.
3. Serve with browned butter and potatoes.

title: Minced Meat Sauce (Sauce til 1 Quart Bouillon)
body:
## Ingredients

- quart of bouillon
- tablespoon of flour
- butter
- bay leaf
- caraway
- white turnips
- small meatballs
- stewed oysters
- capers
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. For one quart of bouillon take one tablespoon of good flour which is undiluted in a piece of butter. This must first be melted in an earthenware and when it begins to boil add the flour and rub it out. It must not become brown at all and it is gradually diluted with broth. After that add bay leaf, caraway, white turnips, small meatballs and whatever stewed oysters, capers or mushrooms one has. If it is for game, pheasant or roasted duck then it is also good to stir a spoon of sweet cream into the sauce when it is to be served.

title: Mincemeat for Mince Pie No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds raisins
- 1 pound currants
- 1 pound beef fat
- 2.5 pounds brown sugar
- 0.5 pound sugar
- 2 tablespoons powdered beef
- 3 lemons with peel and juice
- 1 nutmeg (grated)

## Directions

1. Wine and vinegar according to taste.

title: Mincemeat for Mince Pie No. 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints grated and chopped apples
- 1 pint cooked, chopped beef
- 0.5 pint butter or beef fat
- 1 pint molasses
- 0.5 pint water
- 0.5 tablespoon allspice
- 1 tablespoon cinnamon
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 0.5 tablespoon cloves
- 2.5 large tablespoons vinegar
- 0.5 nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make quickly in the usual way, the form being lined with good dough; a thin layer of filling is added, dough is laid on top as a lid, holes are cut in the lid, and it is baked in a quick oven; it shall be quite light brown, but not hard.

title: Mincemeat Pie No. 1
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds fresh beef
- 1 pound beef fat
- 5 pounds apples
- 2 pounds raisins
- 2 pounds currants
- 1 pound sugar
- 2 tablespoons ground cinnamon
- 1 tablespoon ground nutmeg
- 1 tablespoon pickling spice
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 3 pounds brown sugar
- 1 pint wine
- The broth in which the beef was cooked

## Directions

1. Cook 6 pounds fresh beef tender in scanty water; when it is cold, chop it fine.
2. Chop 1 pound beef fat fine; grate 5 pounds apples and chop them; stone 2 pounds raisins, 2 pounds currants, 1 pound sugar, 2 tablespoons ground cinnamon, 1 tablespoon ground nutmeg, 1 tablespoon pickling spice, 1 tablespoon salt, 3 pounds brown sugar, 1 pint wine, and finally the broth in which the beef was cooked.
3. Mix all together and put it in a stoneware crock with a double layer of paper on top.
4. It must be left at least a week before it is used, and when the pie is prepared, a little more wine and a little more chopped apple is added, what was set aside before.
5. This sort of pie is used mainly for Christmas and other festivities, and they are therefore made in large batches; they can also keep long and be eaten both warm and cold.

title: Mixed Berry Soup (Blaandet Bærsgrød)
body:
## Ingredients

- various berries
- currants
- strawberries
- water
- white sugar
- cornstarch

## Directions

1. Can be made of all kinds of berries, but becomes most palatable made of currants and strawberries together. Currants must be hulled to avoid getting the flavor of the seeds.
2. The most common way is to make it like strawberry soup, but use more cornstarch.
3. The finest version is made this way: Press the berries raw through a fine cloth; when all the juice is extracted, place it over heat with an equal amount of water as juice.
4. When it is warm, stir in sufficient white sugar.
5. Cornstarch is dissolved in lukewarm water, and when the juice is near boiling, stir this in; it need only boil a couple of minutes to thicken, but stir gently so it becomes smooth.
6. Thereafter pour it either into molds or small forms from which it is turned out the next day.
7. Blanched and skinned split almonds look nice sprinkled on fruit pudding.

title: Mixton Marmelade (Mixton Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar
- Fruit
- Peel

## Directions

1. Make it the same way as above, taking half a pound of sugar for each pound of fruit and using only half of the clean peel.

title: Molasses Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup molasses
- 0.5 pound sugar
- 0.5 pound butter
- 0.5 pound water
- 1 tablespoon mace
- 0.5 teaspoon alum dissolved in warm water
- 12 teaspoons saleratus dissolved in molasses
- flour to make dough stiff

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together with flour enough to make a stiff dough, and cut into round cakes.

title: Molasses Candy
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 heaping cups sugar
- 1 cup cider vinegar
- 1 cup molasses water
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 teaspoon saleratus dissolved in a little warm water

## Directions

1. Mix the three first parts together and cook for one hour without stirring
2. Then add butter and soda
3. Handle as the previous recipe

title: Molasses Taffy (Taffel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 part good molasses
- 1 cup vinegar
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 piece butter the size of an egg
- 1 tablespoon saleratus

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar in the vinegar, blend with the molasses, and cook with constant stirring until it becomes hard when a spoonful is dropped in cold water
2. Then stir in the butter and saleratus, the latter dissolved in warm water
3. After this stir once more and pour into greased pans
4. When cooled so it can be handled, cut into squares or, while still soft enough, pull into sticks; for this grease your fingertips with butter

title: Mælkemad (Milk Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk
- Water
- Grains or rice
- Butter
- Salt
- Prepared soda (optional)

## Directions

1. In the preparation of porridge, one must ensure that it boils gently, not fiercely, and without interruption.
2. For milk porridge, place a thick sheet iron in the bottom of the porridge pot, and if the cooking boils over and you must place stronger fire underneath, loosen this sheet, whereby the porridge cooks easily.
3. Over an open fire one can scarcely cook milk porridge.
4. If it is a thin pair of pots you must cook in, it is all the more inclined to scorch, and you must then smear it inside with soft butter and cover it with water; a good remedy against getting milk porridge scorched is to put a little prepared soda in the milk when it is put over the fire.
5. It gives absolutely no taste; about a teaspoon is enough for 3 pints milk.
6. The milk must always boil before the grains are added, and the porridge should not be thinned during cooking; hereby it scorches easily.
7. Rice porridge becomes best when you do not stir it, but then you must never neglect to smear the porridge first.
8. When the milk or water is in a hard boil, the grains are added by the side where it boils strongest and the lid is laid on.
9. The fire must be watched with great care, so that toward the end of cooking it will stand almost still 15 minutes or so before serving, stir in the porridge, and you shall have absolutely no fear, if it seems thin; for by boiling the last quarter under constant stirring, it will become proper.
10. Once you have stirred in the porridge, it must be done regularly.
11. In no kind of milk porridge is salt added before it is taken from the fire.
12. For every quart of milk, take a little heaping tablespoon of cornstarch or groats, which is stirred in some of the cold milk; the remainder is put on the fire, and when it boils, the meal is stirred in, and it boils until the milk is thickened.
13. Two egg yolks are stirred with cold sugar, thinned with warm milk, and poured into the porridge and stirred until it begins to boil; then it is poured into the tureen in which is a little salt and a couple drops vanilla; it tastes good instead of vanilla to boil a couple sticks of hot cinnamon in the milk.
14. You can place boiled eggs cut in half in the tureen; in the milk in which they boil, there must be sugar and a little cinnamon.
15. This milk is then strained and added to the remaining milk.
16. The soup is eaten with small rusks or baked bread, and one usually reckons a quart of milk for 3 persons.

title: Mørt Kjød (Tender Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat or steak
- Batter made of one pound flour
- Beaten eggs
- Two ounces melted butter
- One pint milk
- A little sugar
- Salt

## Directions

1. The meat or steak is cut into quite thin slices and dipped in a batter made of one pound flour, beaten eggs, two ounces melted butter, one pint milk, a little sugar and salt, and fried in butter on a pan.

title: Nøddekage (Walnut Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 3 cups flour
- 1 cup cold water
- 4 eggs
- 2 tablespoons baking powder
- 1 cupful of walnut kernels, chopped and sifted to fine, together with the beaten whites

## Directions

1. Combine ingredients as listed
2. Bake according to standard method

title: Nøddevægge (Almond Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 3 cups flour
- 1 cup cold water
- 4 eggs
- 2 tablespoons baking powder
- 1 cupful of walnut kernels, chopped and fried until crispy, together with the beaten whites

## Directions

1. It is difficult to obtain good butterdough in summer, partly because one usually uses summer butter for it, from which good cannot result, partly because the butter in the heat is too soft to be handled in butterdough.
2. To remedy this, it is proper to keep a crock of fresh butter in a cool place for this use, let the unsalted butter be placed in the night over in cold water, press it out thereof with a spoon, slap it dry in a cloth and keep the dough in a cool place.

title: Omelette with Compote (Omelet med Spiltetøi)
body:
## Ingredients

- ingredients for Simple Omelette
- 2 tablespoons compote

## Directions

1. Make an omelette the same way as Simple Omelette
2. Put 2 tablespoons compote in the middle and finish as the other

title: Onion Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon of chopped onions
- Butter
- 6 tablespoons flour
- Soup (beef or other)
- Toasted bread

## Directions

1. Brown a tablespoon full of chopped onions in butter.
2. Stir in 6 tablespoons of flour.
3. Add the soup gradually, bit by bit, and let it simmer for one hour.
4. Strain it, skim off the fat, and finish it with another seasoning.
5. Serve with toasted bread.

title: Oxefilet Fyld (Stuffed Beef Tenderloin)
body:
## Ingredients

- Thin beef slices
- Fat meat strips
- Pepper
- Ginger
- Butter
- Flour
- Onion
- Anchovy
- Bay leaf
- Whole ginger
- Peppercorns
- Water or broth
- Oysters or shrimp

## Directions

1. Make small incisions in several places on the piece of meat.
2. Take small fat meat strips, dip them in pepper and ginger, and insert them as deeply as possible into the incisions.
3. Close the slits with a small wooden pin or with the fat meat strips, and the meat is larded on the outside.
4. Dust the meat with flour, but not more than can be well rubbed in.
5. Brown it on all sides in butter, using no more butter than is necessary, otherwise the meat will brown unevenly later.
6. When all sides are browned, remove the meat.
7. Brown a good spoonful of flour in the butter remaining in the pan.
8. Add 1½ cups warm water gradually.
9. Return the meat to the pan and add to the sauce some small pearl onions, a couple of anchovies, a small handful of bay leaves, some pieces of whole ginger, and whole peppercorns.
10. Cook everything together under a lid until the meat is tender.
11. Strain the sauce and dilute it if too thick, then add oysters or shrimp if desired, though these are optional.

title: Oxefilet Ryg, Helt Round Steak (Beef Loin, Whole Round Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- Round steak
- Butter
- Onion
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Bay leaf
- Water
- Flour
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut the round steak into pieces as thick as a finger, beat them with a mallet, and chop them a little with a knife.
2. Place a piece of butter on the bottom of a baking dish.
3. Lay a layer of meat on it, sprinkle with a little chopped onion, salt, pepper, and spices, a couple of bay leaves, and a little butter.
4. Add another layer of meat, then the vegetables and butter, and so on.
5. Place it under gentle heat and cook until the meat begins to soften, then gradually add boiling water until it barely covers the meat.
6. When it has cooked for a couple of hours, it is tender.
7. Mix a little flour in a little bouillon or water and add it to the sauce.
8. One can also flour the meat, brown it in butter, add the same vegetables and as much boiling water as to cover the meat, and cook it for a couple of hours until the meat is tender.
9. Many like potatoes with this; these are added, peeled and cut, half an hour before the meal is done.

title: Oxesteg, Tillavet som Mildt (Beef Roast, Prepared Mildly)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Butter
- Water

## Directions

1. Beat a piece of beef well on eight sides.
2. Place it in vinegar overnight.
3. Remove it and dry it, but do not salt it, then lard it with fat meat strips.
4. Brown butter in a pot that fits the meat nicely, then brown the meat in it.
5. Add a little water to the butter and cook it gently until tender.
6. When removed, stir a little flour into the sauce.
7. For hard and lean meat, cut it into long pieces about 2 inches thick and treat them the same way.

title: Oyster Sauce (Østers Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- water or broth
- butter
- flour
- oyster broth
- white wine
- lemon juice
- salt
- nutmeg
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. The beards are removed from the oysters, boiled very briefly in a little water or broth and strained off. A tablespoon of butter is melted, the flour is stirred into it and finished with the strained broth together with white wine, a little lemon juice, salt, nutmeg and as much meat broth that it becomes the right thickness. The oysters are added at the end and should not be boiled but only heated. Very small meatballs can be added which must be boiled in the broth before it is used for the sauce. Cooked and peeled mushrooms can also be added together with the oysters. It is finished with a little egg yolk.

title: Panbroiled Beefsteak (Panbroiled Beef Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- Round Beef
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- potatoes

## Directions

1. The meat should be scraped from the sinews, ensuring that everything is included and only the clear sinews remain, which are set aside.
2. The meat is then formed with the blade of a knife into round, flat patties.
3. Salt and pepper are sprinkled on and they are fried in abundant browned butter; it tastes good to fry some chopped onion at the same time with the meat.
4. Serve with browned butter and potatoes.

title: Parsnip Roulade (Pastinak Rultkål)
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsnip (Salsify) head
- Ground veal forcemeat
- Butter
- Nutmeg
- Salt
- Water
- Sewing twine or cord

## Directions

1. Once the outer leaves of the parsnip head are removed, cut a slice on one side where the stem is.
2. Hollow out the head and fill with ground veal forcemeat, place the slice back on, wrap it with some of the large leaves, and bind it with fine sewing twine or a piece of soft linen.
3. Place it in boiling salted water and cook for 1 to 2 hours depending on the size of the head.
4. Serve with stirred butter with nutmeg in it.
5. The hollowed out part can be cooked with the water and used for vegetable soup or stewed.

title: Peach (Fersken) Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh peaches
- Pie form with good dough
- Sugar
- Peach kernels (three per pie)
- A little water
- Dough for topping

## Directions

1. Peel the fruit, remove the stones, and cut it in pieces.
2. Line a pie form with good dough, and lay the fruit in and sprinkle with sugar.
3. It tastes good to include some peach kernels, three for each pie; a little water is added, and it is baked with a layer of dough on top, like Apple pie.
4. In winter use canned peaches.
5. If desired, apples can be used instead of peaches.

title: Peach Pie with Meringue
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh peaches without stones
- Three egg whites
- Three tablespoons fine sugar
- Butter

## Directions

1. Line the form with fine dough; prepare peaches without stones and fill it.
2. While the pie is in the oven, whip three egg whites with three tablespoons of fine sugar until they can stand alone.
3. When the pie is just baked, take it out, and spread the egg whites on top with a little butter.
4. Set it back in the oven until the egg whites are stiff and browned.
5. They must not be brown. This is the most delicate of all pies.

title: Pear and Apple Compote (Pærer og Æbler)
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- apples
- sugar
- cornstarch

## Directions

1. Peel and core pears and apples and cook together with sugar and a little cornstarch
2. Wash them well and soak them, with water about halfway up
3. Cook them in the same water and add sugar and a little dissolved cornstarch

title: Pickled Cucumber Slices
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen large cucumbers
- vinegar to cover
- 1 pound sugar
- 1 tablespoonful mustard seeds
- 1 ounce ginger
- 1 ounce black pepper
- 1 ounce celery seed
- 1 teaspoonful mace blossoms
- 1 ounce allspice
- 1 ounce cloves
- 1 tablespoonful turmeric
- 1 ounce grated horseradish
- 1 ounce garlic
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Slice 2 dozen large cucumbers and soak them in vinegar for 1 hour, covering them completely
2. Place them in the prepared vinegar
3. For the cold vinegar take: 1 pound sugar, 1 tablespoonful mustard seeds, 1 ounce ginger, 1 ounce black pepper, 1 ounce celery seed, 1 teaspoonful mace blossoms, 1 ounce allspice, 1 ounce cloves, 1 tablespoonful turmeric, 1 ounce grated horseradish, 1 ounce garlic, cayenne pepper
4. Add the cucumbers and simmer for 2 hours
5. It can be used as soon as it is cold

title: Pickled Spice Mixture
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 gallons vinegar
- 1 pint white mustard seed
- 4 ounces ginger
- 3 ounces black peppercorns
- 1 ounce allspice
- 2 ounces cloves
- 1 ounce mace
- 1 ounce nutmeg
- 2 ounces turmeric
- 1 large handful garlic
- 1 handful horseradish
- 4 pounds sugar
- 2 ounces celery seed
- 3 lemons

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together well and expose to the sun for 3 days.

title: Pickled Watermelon Rind
body:
## Ingredients

- watermelon rind with skin and hard sugar
- ginger to taste
- vinegar
- turmeric
- mace blossoms
- cloves
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take the thick rind, scraping off the outer green skin and the inner soft flesh
2. Cut the pieces into small strips and place in strong brine for 10 days
3. Then soak them in fresh water, changing the water daily for 10 days
4. Place them in clear water in a stewpan, heat slowly and boil for 5 minutes
5. Remove and cool in ice water; let them rest overnight
6. Next day, give them a small boiling for 5 minutes in strong brine water
7. Do not boil too hard
8. Remove directly from the brine water and place in water, where they rest for 4 hours
9. Then give another boiling for 5 minutes in fresh water and let them rest in this water overnight
10. Each day thereafter, put enough water over the pieces to cover them, and add enough sugar to make it sweet but not syrup
11. Boil the pieces in this for 10 minutes, drain the water, and spread the pieces on paper to cool
12. Meanwhile prepare a syrup, calculating the same weight of sugar as of rind pieces, ginger according to taste for 1 gallon of pickle, and 1 cup water for every 2 pounds of sugar
13. When the sugar is melted and the syrup hot but not boiling, add the pieces and boil slowly until they are clear
14. Remove and pour back on the forks, while to the syrup add 1 pint vinegar for every pound of sugar, 1 tablespoonful turmeric for 1 gallon of pickle, mace blossoms, cloves and cinnamon to taste
15. Bring to boil, add the pieces and boil for 15 minutes
16. Put in glass jars and seal well

title: Plain Fruit Cake (Enkel Frugtkage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups flour
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup milk
- 3 eggs
- raisins and currants
- 1.5 teaspoons Price's Baking Powder

## Directions

1. Set 2 ounces butter near the fire until it becomes soft without becoming oily
2. Line a baking form on the inside with paper that is smeared with butter
3. Stone 1/2 pound raisins and wash and dry well 1 pound currants
4. Cut 2 ounces sugar into fine small slices
5. Stir 6 ounces sugar to foam with 2 whole eggs; then add 2 others, stir again for a couple of minutes and finally a fifth egg, after which they are stirred a little more
6. Sift 6 ounces flour into it and stir well; then add the butter and fruit, and when it is well mixed, pour it into the form and bake in a moderately warm oven for approximately half an hour
7. It can be tested in the usual manner

title: Poached Eggs à la Crème
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Cream or milk
- Cornstarch
- Butter
- Pepper
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Fill a clean saucepan nearly with boiling water, add a spoonful of vinegar, which must be quite sharp, and a small pinch of salt. Remove the water from the fire and add the broken eggs; when they have cooked three and a half minutes, remove the eggs with a slotted spoon and place on buttered toast on a warm dish. Meanwhile in another bowl half a cup of cream or milk is prepared; if using the latter, it must be thickened with a small amount of cornstarch. Bring it just to boiling point, stirring constantly, so it does not scorch, then add a spoonful of good butter, and a little pepper and salt. It boils up once and is poured over the eggs. It tastes excellent to use half cream and half bouillon.

title: Poached Fish (Kogt Fisk, Vanillemælk)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- water
- cold butter
- pepper
- lemon slices
- fine bread

## Directions

1. Let the fish stand for a few hours in lukewarm water, changing the water several times.
2. Then place it loosely wrapped in cloth in cold water, set it over the fire and let it just come to a boil, then pour off all the water at once and pour boiling water over the fish.
3. Bring this just to a boil, then remove it, dry it and place it on a dish.
4. Add cold butter and pepper to it and place it in the oven for a couple of minutes.
5. Serve with lemon slices and fine bread.

title: Porterhouse Steak (Porterhouse Beef Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- Porterhouse or Sirloin
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- onion
- flour
- stock or bouillon
- pepper

## Directions

1. Have the butcher slice it into thick or thin slices, as desired; thick slices are beaten well on both sides, then sprinkled with salt and pepper, and fried in brown butter.
2. When they are sufficiently cooked, place them on a platter, a piece of butter is added to the pan, in which a tablespoonful of flour is stirred, a little chopped onion and a little ground pepper, after which it is mixed with boiling water.
3. If you have stock, a better sauce is made as follows: when the meat is taken from the pan, butter is added and stirred well with a spoon; when it is brown, chopped onion is added and cooked a little. Then good stock and ground pepper are stirred in.

title: Potetsalat No. 1 (Potato Salad No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salad vegetables
- Butter
- Egg yolk
- Vinegar
- Cream

## Directions

1. Pick salad vegetables so no bad leaves remain; wash well and cut into 7 to 8 pieces.
2. Stir butter into an egg yolk, and add a little vinegar and cream.
3. Place the salad in this and let it rest somewhat before serving.

title: Pound Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 0.5 pound sugar
- 0.5 pound butter
- 4 eggs
- 1 gill rosewater

## Directions

1. Bake on paper and set on a sheet with a teaspoon.

title: Praliné Kugler (Praline Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- same dough as Kokosmakroner
- flour
- more flour than the original dough

## Directions

1. Use the same dough as Kokosmakroner, only take a little more flour in the dough
2. Roll small balls from this, which are baked at good heat
3. You can test when they are done by breaking a ball over

title: Preserved Walnuts for Winter
body:
## Ingredients

- Walnuts
- Salt

## Directions

1. The walnuts must be picked at the right time and be fresh and firm. They are dried in a cloth, peeled and cut fine. In a crock, spread a thin layer of salt, then a layer of walnuts, as thick as three fingers, then a layer of salt, so thick that it covers the walnuts, then walnuts, and so on; the last layer must be salt.
2. It is covered with a piece of cloth, upon which a bundle is laid and upon this a somewhat heavy weight, which after some time has passed, when the walnuts have become firm, can be removed. The walnuts are tied up, placed in a dry place and keep thus until summer.
3. When they are to be used, they are put together with the salt in sufficient boiling water, and cooked until they are soft, then drained through a sieve, the water is pressed out well, and they are rinsed.

title: Pudding (Meat Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ground meat
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. The mold is greased with butter; there must be at least 3 centimeters of space in the mold before it is full, so that the pudding can rise during cooking.
2. It requires about an hour of cooking.
3. It is served either with greens and browned butter or sauce, or also white sauce with white vegetables, asparagus tips and oysters if desired.

title: Pudding Diplomatique (Diplomatic Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound raisins, cleaned and pits removed
- 1 lemon, skin sliced
- 1 pound carrots, peeled and sliced
- an ordinary sponge cake, previously made
- 9 eggs beaten with a pinch of salt, 1 pound sugar, and 1 tablespoon vanilla

## Directions

1. The sponge cake is cut into slices and layered with the fruit in an ordinary buttered mold
2. When everything is used, a custard is made by whisking 9 eggs with a pinch of salt, 1 pound sugar, and 1 tablespoon vanilla
3. This is poured over the pudding; a tablespoon of butter is distributed in small pieces on top
4. The roasting pan is filled with enough boiling water so that it comes two-thirds up the mold, which is then placed in it and cooked gently for one hour
5. Served usually with wine sauce

title: Pæres Føstte i Stuksen (Pears Preserved in a Crock)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe pears of all kinds
- White sugar (1 pound sugar per pound of pears)
- Water (nearly 1 pint brandy)
- Cinnamon stick
- Lemon or lemon juice
- Ground and pounded ginger
- Paper soaked in brandy
- Paper or cloth that fits well to the sides
- Flour and water

## Directions

1. Peel the pears, remove the blossom and a piece of the stem which is scraped off; if the pears are large, cut them over
2. Remove the pear's core and stem, weigh them, and for each pound of pears take 1 pound white sugar, with which layer them in a crock
3. When the crock is filled, pour nearly 1 pint brandy on top
4. Place a board or cloth that fits well to the sides over it, and seal around the edges with a paste of flour and water, stirred to a thick paste, so the steam cannot escape
5. Place the crock in an oven that is not too warm, as needed for roasting a roast, and leave it standing until a little juice comes out, then remove it, check if the seal is broken and set it back in
6. Repeat this for 1 day until the pears are ready
7. The crock remains sealed until use; later seal it well, if possible with a bladder, so the brandy does not lose strength
8. Such preserved fruit keeps very well

title: Rabbit (Rabbit)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rabbit
- Heart and liver of rabbit
- Fat bacon
- Salt

## Directions

1. It is best to have the butcher kill the rabbit, then it is easy to clean it
2. The heart and liver are cooked and chopped finely and mixed with a piece of fat bacon, which is also cut finely, then a stuffing is made like for poultry

title: Ragout af Oxefilet (Beef Tenderloin Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds round steak
- Butter
- Flour
- Carrot
- Onion
- Mushrooms
- Salt
- Pepper
- Water or broth
- Parsley
- Bread

## Directions

1. Cut 2 pounds of round steak into square pieces, two thumbs on each side, and fry them brown in butter.
2. Stir in 2 ounces of flour and brown it.
3. Cut a carrot into pieces about the size of a thumb.
4. Peel and mince three small onions.
5. Add a handful of mushroom slices to the meat along with a tablespoon of salt, a teaspoon of pepper, and enough water to cover the meat.
6. Skim if necessary.
7. Cook until the meat is tender.
8. Remove the meat and check if there is enough salt, adding more as needed.
9. A tablespoon of chopped parsley can be sprinkled over it, or some slices of toasted bread can be arranged around the platter when it is served.

title: Rice Porridge of Milk (Risengryod af Mælk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound rice grains
- Milk
- Salt
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Butter
- Soft beer or malt

## Directions

1. Pick the rice grains, rinse them in cold water and hold them for two days in boiling water.
2. The most important thing is to let them soak for a short time in boiling milk; as the grains are often hard, the teacher notes that the porridge is poured off and the boiling milk extracts the hardness and gives the ready porridge a whiter appearance.
3. For 1 pound rice grains take almost 2 quarts of milk; roast the grains in the boiling milk and cook without stirring according to instructions until they are tender, usually for a little hour.
4. Add salt and eat with sugar, cinnamon and butter, as well as soft beer or malt with it.

title: Rice Porridge of Water (Risengryod af Vand)
body:
## Ingredients

- Water (less than for milk porridge)
- Rice grains
- Butter
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. For this, take a little less water than milk for the previous one, as grains become softer in milk than in water; you can count 2 quarts for the same amount of grains.
2. When it is cooked, stir in a spoonful of butter and while it is hot, add some cinnamon.

title: Rich Omelette No. 1 (Rige Omelet No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flour
- 1 cup clarified butter
- 1 quart milk or good stock
- 10 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
- 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. When 1 cup flour is baked in nearly 1 cup clarified butter, add gradually 1 quart milk or good stock
2. Cook with vigorous stirring and whisking with a spoon
3. Let it cool slightly, then stir vigorously with 10 egg yolks and 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
4. Finally add the 10 beaten egg whites
5. Bake in the oven and serves 8 to 10 people

title: Ripe Tomatoes (Modne Tomater)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Scald and scrape the tomatoes; then cut them up and cook them tender, after which they are stewed with butter, pepper and salt.

title: Risengrynsbage (Rice Pudding Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound rice grains
- 8 pints milk
- 4 ounces butter
- 9 egg yolks
- 1 pound chopped almonds and a couple of bitter ones
- clean lemon peel
- sugar and salt to taste
- beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. 1 pound rice grains is shelled and cooked to thick porridge in 8 pints milk.
2. Then it is stirred warm with 4 ounces butter, 9 egg yolks, 1 pound chopped almonds and a couple of bitter ones, clean lemon peel, sugar and salt to taste, finally the beaten whites.
3. The dough is poured into the greased and strewn form, sprinkled with sugar and baked in an hour's time.
4. Instead of almonds, vanilla, cinnamon and sugar can be added.

title: Risgrød Pudding (Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces rice
- 4½ tablespoons sugar
- 1 tablespoon lemon extract
- 3 quarts milk

## Directions

1. Rinse rice well in two waters and pour into a mold that can be made of tin
2. Add 4½ tablespoons sugar and 1 tablespoon lemon extract
3. Pour 3 quarts milk over and place in a moderately warm oven to bake for 1.5 hours until it is in a creamy consistency

title: Roasted Chicken with Stuffing (Summer Roasted Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young chicken
- Parsley
- Butter
- Cold butter
- Bread
- Sugar
- Salt
- Flour

## Directions

1. There is a skin on the young chicken's sides that must be removed before cooking
2. Wash the chicken
3. Decorate it with parsley by inserting one end at the side of the other down along the backbone, taking chopped and washed parsley with a little butter and pushing it with a finger down into the heart hole so a little parsley sticks up
4. The cavity can be filled with parsley and butter or with stuffing made as follows: Soak a few slices of French bread in milk, let it stand covered for an hour and break it into pieces with a spoon
5. Stir 1 piece of cold butter until creamy, then blend in 1-2 oz of bread, a little sugar, and salt
6. When well mixed, pour it into the cavity after it is opened from one side so you can get a hand in and with this loosen the skin as high as possible on the roast
7. If there are holes in the cavity, sew them together, and when the dough is in the cavity, sew it closed
8. Dust the roast with flour and roast for one hour
9. Summer roast is handled the same as young roast, but only allow 15 minutes per pound

title: Roasted Chicken, American Style (Roasted Chicken, American Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken's ribcage
- 1 small onion
- 1/2 carrot
- 1/2 parsnip
- 3 sprigs parsley
- 1 sprig thyme
- 3 bay leaves
- Salt
- 1/2 pint hot water
- Butter
- 1 tablespoon potato starch
- 2 tablespoons cold water
- Cloves
- 1 piece sugar

## Directions

1. Have the butcher prepare the ribcage by removing the bones and holding it flat with the help of a silver string tied around it
2. Place the roast on a roasting pan on a bed of vegetables: 1 small onion, 1/2 carrot, 1/2 parsnip, 3 sprigs parsley, 1 sprig thyme, and 3 bay leaves
3. Do not add water to the roasting pan
4. Do not salt until the surface has shrunk
5. When making the sauce, remove the vegetables and add 1/2 pint hot water to the roasting pan, boiling it quickly together with the drippings for a couple of minutes until the sauce is thick enough
6. Thicken the sauce with 1 tablespoon potato starch dissolved in 2 tablespoons cold water before stirring it into the sauce
7. Add cloves and a piece of sugar to the sauce
8. The roast becomes more tender and flavorful when basted
9. If desired, you can stud it with half the amount
10. Brown some butter in a pan and blend with flour to make a roux, then pour over the roast after it has been browned in butter and baste it often

title: Roasted Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- pepper
- cold butter

## Directions

1. Clean, wash and dry the fish.
2. Sprinkle a little fine salt and pepper over it, place it skin-side down on a grid and roast it over clear, bright coals.
3. When it is nicely brown, turn it for a short time, then remove it and spread cold butter over it.
4. A little marrow under the fish enhances its flavor.

title: Roasted Pheasant (Roasted Pheasant)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pheasant
- Salt
- Salted or smoked bacon
- Thin gray paper
- Fat
- Hot water
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Pheasant requires at least 25 minutes roasting time per pound and must not be red inside
2. After plucking, rub the roast very strongly with salt, which helps the skin become crisp, and cut it so the entire skin is in small strips
3. The oven must be very hot when the roast is placed in so the skin becomes crisp
4. The skin can also be cut off together with a piece of hot fat if the skin is very fat
5. Place the roast in to become warm and take it out again while it is sprinkled with heated breadcrumbs mixed with roasted chestnuts, peppers, and salt, then place it back in the oven and roast it
6. It is always better for safety to put some hot water on the roast when it is placed in
7. First wash the roast well in lukewarm water, then stuff it with salt or smoked bacon
8. Rub a strip of thin gray paper rubbed in fat and place it over
9. Place the roast on the roasting pan with a little hot fat water over
10. The fire must be very good and steady
11. 20 minutes per pound is never too much for pheasant
12. Half an hour before you think it is done, remove the paper and for most it will be necessary to test the roast
13. If a meat fork with certainty goes through to the bone at the thickest spot, the heat is applied in all the ways you can and the roast is basted very diligently
14. In the last half hour it becomes beautifully brown
15. An excellent sauce is made as follows: from the raw roast cut off all irregular or unnecessary pieces to give the roast a more rounded appearance
16. It is good if you can get the meat this way in a little hot water with 1/2 nutmeg, a few cloves, a little salt, and a small teaspoon of cayenne pepper
17. It boils slowly until it has reduced to half
18. You must skim it as soon as foam appears
19. It is then strained and when the sauce from the water is skimmed, it is blended with it
20. 1 tablespoon butter is browned in a pan, with which 1/2 tablespoon flour is blended, and all the sauce is now stirred with it
21. If it should be too thick, it can be thinned with boiling water
22. You can also make it using only butter, flour, and the sauce from the roasting pan, plus boiling water if necessary
23. Lingonberry jelly is always served with pheasant

title: Roasted Veal Breast (Stekt Kalvebryst)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds veal breast
- 1 pound beef
- 1 large tablespoon flat bread crumbs
- 2 eggs
- a little pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- half a small pinch of powdered or grated cinnamon
- brown butter
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Mince the veal breast and beef finely
2. Stir in one large tablespoon of flat bread crumbs, 2 eggs, a little pepper, salt, nutmeg, and half a small pinch of powdered or grated cinnamon
3. Place the veal breast in a deep roasting pan with browned butter and brown it well on both sides
4. Pour bouillon over it and cook slowly in the oven for approximately 2 hours
5. Turn it occasionally during cooking

title: Root Vegetable Soup (Mixed Root Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- About 20 pieces of parsnip
- Carrots
- A short piece of marrow bone, cut into 4 pieces
- 4 to 6 tablespoons of potato starch or wheat flour (optional)
- Salt

## Directions

1. After the soup is skimmed, add about 20 pieces of parsnip, carrots, and a short piece of marrow bone cut into 4 pieces.
2. When both parts are tender, remove them; if a smooth soup is desired, stir 4 to 6 tablespoons of unbeaten potato starch or wheat flour into the soup and let it boil until the meat is tender and the soup is smooth.
3. After this is done, set it back on the fire together with the carrots and marrow bone, which are cut into smaller pieces and get a good finishing.
4. Serve with toasted bread.

title: Roulade (Meat-Filled Cabbage Roll)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage leaves
- ground meat
- string
- beef stock or water
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Half-boil tender white cabbage leaves, and when they are cold, take ground meat and roll it in them; the thickness should be like a large meatball, and the length 3 inches.
2. They are tied together with string, cooked in beef stock or water, which is salted a little, and browned afterwards on the outside in butter.
3. The string cannot be removed until they are ready to be served.
4. They are served with browned butter.

title: Roulettes (Meat Roulettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds calf liver
- 1 pound fresh meat from the liver
- 1 pound raw Medister sausage
- 4 anchovies
- several raw egg yolks
- black pepper
- vinegar
- salt
- a little thyme
- a little grated rye bread
- a few teaspoons of mustard
- cream
- 6 to 8 eggs
- approximately 1 pint flour
- bacon slices
- smoked bacon

## Directions

1. Scrape and grate the calf liver through a sieve
2. Mince fresh meat from the liver and mix with the liver, raw Medister sausage, and anchovies removed from bones
3. Mix well with raw egg yolks, black pepper, vinegar, salt, a little thyme, a little grated rye bread, a few teaspoons of mustard, cream, and 6 to 8 eggs
4. In a form with a firm bottom, place a layer of forcemeat, then sliced roasted meat, then another layer of forcemeat, alternating layers
5. Top layer should be forcemeat covered with pieces of fat and smoked bacon to prevent browning
6. Remove bacon layer when baked
7. Bake for 1.5 hours
8. Serve warm with sharp, brown sauce, though most commonly served cold for open-faced sandwiches without sauce

title: Roulettes of Lobster
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster
- butter
- flour
- bouillon
- boiled, finely sliced meat from lobster
- black pepper
- salt
- bread crumbs
- eggs
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Cook two raw egg yolks in butter
2. Bake 12 ounces flour in the butter and gradually add 1 pint bouillon
3. Cook until thick, then mix in 1 pound boiled, finely sliced lobster meat, black pepper, and salt
4. When completely cold, take small portions and roll in bread crumbs
5. Dip in beaten egg, then in bread crumbs
6. Cook in clarified fat together with a little fat for cooking
7. For this portion, which makes approximately 16 roulettes, use 5 tablespoons butter and 4 eggs
8. Can be served with sharp sauce and stewed potatoes or with Semper

title: Roulettes of Shrimp
body:
## Ingredients

- shrimp
- butter
- flour
- salt
- nutmeg
- sugar
- bouillon or milk

## Directions

1. Cook shrimp
2. Remove meat from shells and cut into slivers
3. Brown shells in butter, spread over a pan, and add water
4. After cooking 1 quarter hour, strain it, and the shrimp butter is set aside on the side with flour, a little nutmeg and sugar, and thinned with bouillon until it becomes a porridge
5. Stir in shrimp meat and a little salt
6. When cold, cook as directed above
7. Shells can also be cooked with milk; shrimp butter is then thinned with cream or good milk

title: Rundstykker (Bread Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- ½ pound sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 quart milk
- 2 cents yeast
- Flour
- Beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir together 1 cup butter well with ½ pound sugar; then with 4 egg yolks
2. Warm 1 quart milk, dissolve 2 cents yeast in it, mix it in and stir in as much flour as will make the dough a suitable consistency (somewhat thicker than for hand-pancakes); fold in the beaten egg whites
3. Beat it well in a bowl, which covered stands by the heat for a couple of hours, so the dough can rise
4. When the pan is hot, add a little butter in each hollow, and fill the roll a little more than halfway; when they are baked enough on one side, turn them with two spoons

title: Rye Bread Soup (Rye Bread Gruel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound rye bread
- 3 quarts water
- lemon zest
- cinnamon
- 1 glass white wine
- juice of two lemons
- sugar
- 1 half pint cream

## Directions

1. Cut the rye bread finely, cook it in 3 quarts of water until it is smooth, strain it through a sieve, and if this is not fine, then also through a strainer, and set it again with lemon zest and cinnamon.
2. When the soup boils, add a glass of white wine, juice of two lemons and sugar.
3. Whip half a pint of cream to foam in the tureen, and pour the soup over it while whisking with the whisk.
4. If you do not have cut bread to use, the bread can be grated, and then the soup does not need to be strained.

title: Rød Frukteddike på Flasket (Red Fruit Vinegar in Bottles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good ripe berries, freshly picked
- Brandy

## Directions

1. Crush and strain the berries in a cloth into completely dry bottles
2. Pour enough brandy over so it stands a couple inches above the juice
3. Bottles should be small, as the juice does not keep once opened
4. Seal and cork

title: Rød Ørred (Red Trout)
body:
## Ingredients

- Trout
- Water
- Salt
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Clean, wash and dry very carefully.
2. Then wrap it in a cloth that fits well, with the fish, and place it in a fish kettle, covered with salted, cold water.
3. It cooks in an hour or more depending on its size.
4. Season with cream sauce like the previous one, only of course without fish sauce.

title: Rødbeder (Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound carrots (dark red)
- 4 lemons
- 1 pound sugar
- vinegar (from cooking the carrots)
- additional sugar for syrup
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Select dark red carrots best found in August. Scrape clean, cut into slices thin as straw, then cut these into equally thin strips and weigh them. For 1 pound carrots, take 4 lemons, peel very thinly so no white pith remains, and cut the peel into fine, thin strips. Place both carrots and lemon peel in cold water on the fire and bring to a boil with lid on for about half an hour, or until carrots are tender (but must not fall apart). Drain through a sieve.
2. For 1 pound carrots, take 1 pound sugar and boil it to a thin syrup with the water in which the carrots were cooked. Add the carrots with the juice of 2 lemons and boil for approximately 10 minutes. Place in a crock and let stand a couple of days, then drain so the syrup can drip off. Boil this syrup for 8-10 minutes with 3 ounces sugar and more lemon juice to taste. Return the carrots (but do not boil them further). Pack with parchment paper and tie up.

title: Rødbeder (Red Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- Red beets
- Vinegar
- Water
- Butter
- Salt
- Sugar
- Cherry juice or currant juice

## Directions

1.
