The outer leaves and stems are removed from red beets, which are cut into fine strips and placed on the fire with some vinegar, from which it becomes red, and a little water, and cooked with a lid over a gentle fire. If the beets dry out, more vinegar or bouillon is added to it, but not more than to make the beets moist. When tender, butter or fat from beef bouillon is added to it, and finally sugar, a little cherry or currant juice and salt. The sauce can be thickened.

title: Rødbederrød (Redcurrant Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- Redcurrants
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. This is made like crowberry porridge.
2. If you want it for a roast, it is poured off carefully, and not thickened, but only boiled in very little water and sufficient sugar.
3. In all cases, the stems are picked by taking hold with a fork in the red end and thereby pulling the skin off, and cutting the stems into small pieces.

title: Rødspættesuppe (Red Mullet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large carrots
- 1 celery
- 2 parsnips
- 1 small parsley root
- 4 potatoes of medium size
- 1 parsnip
- 2 ounces butter
- Salt
- a few whole peppercorns
- 2.5 quarts water

## Directions

1. Cut all the roots into strips approximately one inch long and the thickness of a finger
2. After the potatoes are peeled, drain the water from them on a kitchen towel
3. Place 2 ounces of butter on the stove; when it is melted, add the roots and cook for a quarter hour with constant stirring so it does not brown
4. Then add salt, a few whole peppercorns and 2.5 quarts water, though a quart of water can be used instead, cooking intermittently
5. The whole soup must cook for about two hours, and to prevent it from boiling over, it must cook evenly and covered
6. If you find there are too many roots, some can be held back at serving
7. Serve with toasted bread and is for 3 to 4 persons

title: Sago Pudding (Sagogrød)
body:
## Ingredients

- sago
- water
- fruit juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. The best large sago grains are particularly suited to sago pudding, in which no thickening is needed.
2. If you get the best sago that swells up, you can reckon 1 pound sago to 4-5 quarts water.
3. Juice and sugar are added according to taste.
4. The sago should cook soft in the water before juice and sugar are added, and thereafter the whole gets a good boiling.
5. Pour a spoonful onto a plate to see if it is the right thickness.
6. If it is too thick, it can be thinned with a little boiling water, but if it is too thin, there is nothing to do but smooth it with a spoonful of cornstarch stirred in cold water.
7. The best pearl sago can cook out to a clear whole, and it becomes best with juice; but if you prefer it, it can also be made with stewed fruit and lemon juice.
8. If the berries are unripe and very sour, they should stand a while and draw themselves in warm water, which is then drained off and they are placed over heat in fresh water.
9. To 2 quarts berries take ½ pint water. Hull the berries and cook them well, stirring often from the bottom, after which the pudding is finished with sugar and cinnamon.
10. For gooseberry pudding, brown sugar can be used, but white sugar gives finer taste and more beautiful appearance.

title: Sago Soup (Sago Gruel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds large sago grains
- 1 half lemon, zest
- 1 half cup raisins
- 3 eggs
- white sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 glass white wine

## Directions

1. Rinse the sago grains in cold water and strain in boiling water.
2. When it has cooked until the grains are swollen from the water, add the zest of half a lemon together with half a cup of raisins.
3. Stir 3 eggs into the tureen with white sugar, then with the juice of a lemon and 1 glass white wine.
4. When the grains are clear, they are cooked, and the soup is poured boiling over the eggs while stirring constantly in the tureen.
5. This is a wholesome and tasty diet and the soup is cooked in half an hour. The wine can be omitted.

title: Sago Soup with Almonds (Sago Gruel with Almonds)
body:
## Ingredients

- sago grains
- candied fruit
- blanched almonds
- cream
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Prepare it like the aforementioned soup, but instead of eggs and wine, add candied fruit and, if desired, blanched almonds.
2. Fruit, whole spices, if at the beginning, so the soup can come out in its own serving dish.

title: Salat No. 2 (Salad No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons mustard
- 2 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Grind mustard and hard-boiled egg yolks together in oil; add vinegar, pepper and a pinch of salt.
2. Prepare the salad as before.

title: Salt Walnuts for Winter
body:
## Ingredients

- Walnuts
- Vinegar
- Shallots
- Onion
- Ginger
- Young peppercorn
- Mace
- Allspice berries
- Mustard seed
- Pepper
- Allspice

## Directions

1. The walnuts must be picked at the same time and be of the same size as the others. They are placed in a cold brine, which is so strong that it can bear an egg, and which must be boiled and skimmed well. They stay in it for 6 days, are taken up and placed in a fresh and equally strong, cold brine, in which they stay again for 6 days.
2. They are drained in a sieve, where the brine is well pressed out, and placed in a crock. Then as much vinegar as is needed is put over the fire so that it can stand completely over the walnuts; into this is put for 100 walnuts: 4 shallots, a little piece of an onion, 3/4 pieces of ginger, 2 pieces of young peppercorn, 5 to 6 pieces of mace, 10 allspice berries, 6 spoonfuls of mustard seed, 2 spoonfuls of pepper and 2 spoonfuls of allspice.
3. When all this has cooked, it is poured off, covered with a cloth and poured cold over the walnuts, which are then bound up very well, as air would make them soft. After the passage of a year they are ready for use and keep themselves for several years. They are quite black in color, but give a good taste in sauce, especially in butter sauce for fish.

title: Sauce for Milk (Sauce til Mælk)
body:
## Ingredients

- cream
- cold butter
- good egg yolk

## Directions

1. One puts cream, cold butter and some good egg yolk together on the side and stirs until it boils and becomes even.

title: Sauce for Roasted Poultry (Sauce til Ristbrøftet)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- beef broth
- sugar
- currants
- butter
- flour
- bone broth
- currants
- redcurrant jelly

## Directions

1. The ordinary sauce is the one that forms itself in the earthenware when one rubs the roast well before it goes into the brown butter, and adds whole pieces of juniper, whole peppercorns and whole onions which are strained off. But some prefer a brown and cold sauce, and then less wine is put on the roast. One makes a little browned roux as for Madeira sauce, thins it with broth and adds sugar and currants. Then either peppercorns or mushrooms are used, only a little juniper. One also uses butter and flour as for brown sauce and thins with bone broth. When the flour is well browned and one has had soup in it once, take a couple of tablespoons of sugar which is dissolved in the rest. Then add more broth and finally redcurrant jelly. This sauce is used for grilled meat.

title: Scrambled Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Cream or milk
- Butter
- Salt
- Fine-chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Beat 8 eggs well, first alone, then with 8 small spoonfuls of cream or good milk; thereafter put it in the saucepan over the stove with an ounce of butter and a little salt. It tastes good to add a little finely-chopped parsley; there must not be any liquid and one does not stir, but scrapes continuously from the bottom with a scraper; if one stirs around, the pieces will break apart and that is not the intention. When you find them sufficiently cooked, take them off the fire. They must not be too hard. You can add some boiled asparagus tips to them. Count 3 eggs per person.

title: Simple Cream (Simpel Creme)
body:
## Ingredients

- Whipped cold cream
- Fine sugar or a little vanilla
- Or marmalade
- Fruit juice (when cooked with sugar and cooled before adding to the whipped cream)

## Directions

1. Whipped cold cream is sweetened with either fine sugar or a little vanilla
2. It can also be sweetened with marmalade
3. Fruit juice can also be used when it is cooked with the sugar and becomes cold again before it is added to the cream; but there should not be added more than what just gives a fine taste and a light red color

title: Simple Omelette (Simpel Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- egg whites
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 pinch pepper
- 1 piece butter the size of a walnut

## Directions

1. Put the egg yolks in one bowl and the egg whites in another
2. Add 1 tablespoon salt and 1 pinch pepper, and beat for half a minute
3. Put a piece of butter the size of a walnut in a clean omelette or frying pan and place over heat to warm
4. Gently stir the beaten egg whites into the yolks, and pour into the pan
5. Run a spatula along the bottom of the pan so the omelette folds together into a ball in the middle
6. When it is cooked enough, slide it onto a warm plate held close to it
7. Serve immediately

title: Sitron (Lemon)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lemons
- Sugar (1 pound sugar per 1 pint juice)

## Directions

1. All the inside is removed from the lemons and pressed through a sieve or wrung out in a cloth.
2. For 1 pint of juice take 1 pound of sugar, it is put all at once on the stove, cooked and skimmed well in 10 minutes, poured into bottles and corked.

title: Slices of French Bread Fried in Butter (Pain Perdu)
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of French bread
- Butter
- White sugar

## Directions

1. Dip the slices of French bread in milk and fry them in butter on both sides.
2. When fried on both sides, pour the batter over and bake in good fire, not held back by the flames, until it shines on top.
3. It does not need to be turned, and is arranged with white sugar on top.

title: Slices of French Bread in Mustard Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of French bread
- Milk
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Butter

## Directions

1. Dip slices of French bread in milk, cover them for a short time, then turn in sugar and cinnamon and fry in butter on both sides.
2. Then cover them with a good fruit jelly and arrange.

title: Small Cakes (Småfager)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- equal amount butter
- equal amount sugar
- equal amount flour
- vanilla or almond extract

## Directions

1. Beat eggs, and take equal amounts of butter, sugar and flour. Mix well together, and a little vanilla or almond extract can be added. Place on a greased sheet with a teaspoon. They should be light brown at the edges.

title: Smelte Fedt (Rendered Lard)
body:
## Ingredients

- All fat trimmed from the rind
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut all the fat from the rind and cut it into small pieces afterwards.
2. Fill a pot with it and add a cupful of water to prevent the skin from burning.
3. Melt it very slowly over low heat, not too strong, stirring occasionally with small movements.
4. This continues until the meat is reduced to quite small, dry pieces.
5. These are then taken up with a spoon; a little salt is now added.
6. When the fat is clear, strain it either through a sieve or a large cloth.
7. If the latter is thickened by the fat, dip it in hot water.
8. As long as the clear fat runs through, the cloth must not be pressed, and when this is necessary, it is best to press this fat into another pot for simpler use.
9. What you mainly have to be careful about is not to burn the fat.
10. During the simmering stir constantly, and after the salt has been added, put the pot back where it is less hot.
11. It is best to bind cloths over the handles; otherwise you can use paper, and over it a cloth dipped in melted fat.

title: Smoked Eel Salad (Røget Ål Salat)
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked eel
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- shallot

## Directions

1. Beat and skin the smoked eel from the bones; soak it for half an hour in cold milk, rinse in water and dry in a cloth
2. Cut into very small pieces and prepare with fine oil, vinegar, pepper and chopped shallot

title: Smoked Pork Sauce (Røget Svinekød Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked pork
- lard
- carrot
- onion
- dry flour
- bouillon or water
- ground pork
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- wine

## Directions

1. A pound of smoked pork sliced into small pieces the size of a thumb is fried in one pound of lard. As soon as it is brown, add two pounds of carrot sliced in layers and as much onion, likewise sliced in layers. Two pounds of dry flour are stirred in and browned. After that a quart of bouillon is added. If you do not have that, then one quart of water and one pound of finely chopped meat. It is seasoned with a tablespoon of salt, a little pepper and a bay leaf. It is simmered slowly for one hour and skimmed properly. After that it is strained and a glass of wine can be added. This sauce can keep over a week in a cold place and is used for all kinds of dark meat, game or light fish.

title: Små Kager (Small Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 whole eggs
- 1 pound soft sugar
- 1 pound flour
- 1 ounce ground cinnamon
- one spoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat 6 whole eggs, one at a time, with 1 pound soft sugar until they are light
2. Then stir 1 pound flour, 1 ounce ground cinnamon and one spoon baking powder into it
3. They are placed with a spoon in small strips on the cold pan, which is first buttered with butter or lard; and baked at good heat
4. These cakes need strong heat to raise quickly, but must absolutely not become brown, and if the oven is therefore too slow and gives disproportionately strong top heat, then one does better not to bake them; the oven door must not be opened until the baking is done and with baking generally takes a little half hour

title: Små Patentcet med Gummitragant (Small Patent Cakes with Gum Tragacanth)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg white
- 5 cents gum tragacanth
- soft sugar
- 2 ounces finely ground sweet almonds

## Directions

1. The evening before the cakes are to be baked, pour 1 egg white over 5 cents gum tragacanth, which stands until the next morning
2. Then stir it well and then stir with as much soft sugar as it can take and 2 ounces finely ground sweet almonds
3. Small pieces of dough are rolled with the hand against the pastry board into long strips, cut as for pralines and baked light brown on the buttered pan

title: Små Ål (Small Eels)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eels
- Water
- Salt
- Parsley
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. When they are skinned and the fins are cut off, wash them very carefully and dry well.
2. Then cut them into pieces of a finger's length, and put them in a pot with just enough cold water that it stands over them, a little salt is added, as well as chopped parsley, and it cooks slowly, covered, in a good time.
3. Then add a large spoonful of butter and a bit of flour stirred in as little cold water as possible.
4. Arrange in a deep dish. The small eels are the best.

title: Smørløber (Butter Runners)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound dry rubber sugar
- 1 pound flour
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Clarify 1 pound butter and stir until it is light with 1 pound dry rubber sugar, 1 pound flour and 1 egg
2. When it is too hard to stir, knead it with the hands until it releases the fingers
3. Roll with the hands as meal into small round cakes, flatten them, place on the pan and bake quite light

title: Snitte Fisk-Pudding (Sliced Fish Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- 1 bread roll
- salt
- pepper
- 1 tablespoon minced parsley
- 2 pounds fish
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes in well salted water
2. Soak a small piece of bread in cold water and wring dry in a clean cloth
3. Stir in salt, pepper and 1 tablespoon minced parsley
4. Clean 2 pounds fish, slice into thin pieces and lay in cold salt water
5. When potatoes are cooked, peel, mash through a sieve and season with pepper and salt
6. Butter a pudding mold in the bottom with butter and lay a layer of bread and a layer of fish alternately in it
7. Lay a neat layer of potato mixture on top and bake for 1 hour in a not too hot oven

title: Snow Eggs (Snebolge)
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg whites
- Egg yolk
- For each 4 egg whites, a little tablespoonful of current or gooseberry jelly

## Directions

1. Only egg whites and egg yolk are used
2. For each 4 egg whites, take a little tablespoonful of currant or gooseberry jelly
3. Beat it all together for a long time, even after it has become hard foam, as it thereby becomes whiter and finer in appearance
4. From four egg whites comes a fairly large portion
5. Place it in a tall cup on a dish where there is enough cold cream so that the cup can float in it
6. Eat it with small dry cakes

title: Snøfrisk Brød (Ginger Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup cold water
- 1 tablespoon ginger
- 1 teaspoon ground clove
- 1 tablespoon baking soda, dissolved in boiling water
- wheat flour as needed to make suitable dough

## Directions

1. Melt the butter, warm the molasses slightly, add spices and sugar, and whisk for ten minutes
2. Then add the water, soda and flour
3. Stir very well and bake in three small loaves
4. Serve half fresh

title: Soft Baked Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Salt
- Butter
- Bread or buttered toast

## Directions

1. In a buttered mold, which must be suitable for holding them, a few eggs are broken. A little salt is sprinkled on, and a small piece of butter is placed on each yolk. Bake in the oven until the white is set. Serve hot with butter and bread or buttered toast.

title: Soufflé Omelette (Omelette Soufflée)
body:
## Ingredients

- egg yolks
- egg whites
- powdered sugar
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. For each egg, take 1 tablespoon powdered sugar and half a tablespoon flour
2. Beat the yolks first, then with the sugar, then add flour and finally the beaten whites
3. This is not fried in fat like other omelettes, but in a frying pan covered with handkerchiefs over the entire pan, or in the oven in forms lined with stiff paper and bent to the shape of the mold
4. The forms should be about 3 fingers high; 2 fingers dough is put in
5. Grease the forms well with butter and sprinkle with breadcrumbs, and put butter in the frying pan
6. If baked in the oven and it gives too much top heat, it is best to put paper over, as they must not become too brown, which they tend to do
7. They are served cold and placed one on another with compote between
8. On top they can be topped with egg foam and compote

title: Soufflé Omelette with Rich Filling (Rigt Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons powdered sugar
- juice and zest of 1 lemon
- 6 egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat 6 egg yolks for half an hour with 6 tablespoons powdered sugar, and also juice and zest of 1 lemon
2. Beat the whites stiff and stir in carefully
3. Grease the mold with butter, pour the batter in and let it stand for 5 minutes over heat to rise
4. Then put it in a hot oven and bake for 1.5 hours
5. It cannot be taken from the mold and is served with hot butter

title: Sour Milk Omelette (Suur Fløde Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint sour milk
- 1 pint sweet milk
- 6 egg yolks
- a little powdered sugar
- 6 tablespoons flour
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Whip together 1 pint sour milk and 1 pint sweet milk into foam
2. Then add 6 egg yolks, a little powdered sugar, and 6 tablespoons flour
3. Stir in the beaten egg whites when the batter is poured into the oven

title: Spicecake (Krydderkake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 5 eggs
- 1 cup molasses
- 3 cups milk
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup currants
- 1 teaspoon Price's Baking Powder
- 1 nutmeg
- a little sugar
- cinnamon
- allspice
- and 1 glass brandy

## Directions

1. Stir the butter and sugar as usual first, then the beaten egg yolks and stir well before half of the flour is added
2. then add the raisins, the rest of the flour together with the stiffly beaten egg whites, and finally the brandy
3. This cake must bake for a couple of hours and can keep for several months
4. Bake in a hot oven

title: Spinatsuppe (Spinach Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts spinach
- Salted water
- 2 quarts milk
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 1 small teaspoon salt
- A little white pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook spinach in slightly salted water; when it is completely soft so it can be drawn apart easily, drain the water through a sieve
2. Chop the spinach finely and rub through the sieve
3. Cook 2 quarts milk, stir the spinach in it, and when it is near boiling season it with 1 tablespoon cornstarch mixed in cold milk; season with a little teaspoon salt, a little white pepper and nutmeg
4. It must be served as soon as it boils up

title: Steffani Kager (Steffani Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fine butter
- 1 pound powdered sifted sugar
- 4 eggs
- 10 drops vanilla
- 1 pound moistened potato flour

## Directions

1. Beat 1 pound fine butter until creamy with 1 pound powdered sifted sugar
2. First then add one by one 4 eggs, 10 drops vanilla, scraped as finely as possible, and 1 pound moistened potato flour
3. Place on the buttered pan with a spoon and bake light yellow
4. These cakes are delicious, but one must not spare effort in stirring the dough

title: Stegt Hviidkaal (Braised Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Butter
- Sugar
- Water
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. The outer and hardest leaves of white cabbage are cooked until tender in water with a little soda; this is discarded, and a little bouillon, some butter, nutmeg and a little salt are added, with which it is cooked until tender.
2. Browned potatoes can be placed on top if sauce and mushrooms are lacking.

title: Stegt Høns (Roasted Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Water
- Bread crumbs
- Finely minced onion
- Powdered sage
- Melted butter
- Pepper
- Grated nutmeg
- Salt
- Eggs
- Mel (flour)
- Parsley
- Celery
- Lemon or plum
- Butter
- Flour
- Water
- Cherry liqueur

## Directions

1. As soon as the chicken is slaughtered and cold, have a pot full of boiling water ready.
2. Put the chicken in cold water for half an hour; then take hold of one hind leg and rotate it carefully around the boiling water, as long as until the hairs can be pulled off in handfuls.
3. Then take it up, dry it, and with a brush or a very coarse cloth, brush it from head to tail until the skin is quite clean.
4. Then cut it open, take out the entrails, first rinse with lukewarm water, then with thin soda water, and finally with plenty of cold salted water.
5. Dry it inside afterwards.
6. Now wrap it in a damp cloth and keep it damp until you are ready to fill it.
7. For filling, take a cupful of bread crumbs, half a cup of finely minced onion, two tablespoons of powdered sage, three tablespoons of melted butter, half a tablespoon of pepper, a quarter tablespoon of grated nutmeg, half a teaspoon of salt and two well-beaten eggs.
8. Everything except the eggs is mixed well together; then the eggs are stirred in, and the whole is filled into the chicken so it gets a natural shape.
9. Sew it up, bend the forelegs backwards, the hind legs forwards, under the body and close to it; they can be held in place with string.
10. Dry it well now and brush it with flour.
11. Put it in the oven with a small amount of fat and water, but salt it first later.
12. Three times drip it with butter and water, later with broth.
13. If you want the skin soft and tender, rub it every fifth minute or so with a cloth dipped in melted butter, after it has begun to steam.
14. An old hen will usually roast for two hours or so on that, if the oven is good and hot.
15. To serve: On the platter lay a thick bed of parsley, upon which the chicken kneels; here and there in the bed arrange stalks of the freshest celery that rise above the rest, a wreath of parsley around the neck, and in the mouth give it either a lemon or a piece of pineapple; use two sauceboats for the sauce.
16. For the sauce, brown a lump of butter; in it brown two tablespoons of flour, the sauce from the pan is stirred in after first being carefully skimmed, boiling water is added if it is too thick, and when it is taken off the heat, add the juice of half a lemon and half a small glass of cherry liqueur.
17. When serving, cut the breast off first; then slice the thighs, skin and legs are removed and the ribs are loosened.
18. The filling is taken on a small dish and served around the table.
19. With this, mushrooms or whatever else matches.

title: Stewed Apple Omelette (12 Pore Sabler)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large apples
- a little water
- 1/2 lb butter
- 1/2 lb powdered sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- egg whites
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Peel 12 large apples, cut in pieces, remove the cores, and cook to pulp with a little water underneath
2. When cooked, stir in 1/2 lb butter and 1/2 lb powdered sugar, and when cooled, stir in 4 egg yolks and finally the beaten whites
3. Line the mold or tin with breadcrumbs and bake light brown
4. Serves about 3 people, served warm with powdered sugar

title: Stewed Beef (Oxebryst)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds beef brisket
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Marjoram
- Minced onion
- Parsley
- Soda or catsup
- Flour

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into pieces about one inch long and place them in a stewing pot with just enough water to cover them, and cook gently for 2 hours.
2. Set it aside until the next morning, then season it with salt, pepper, marjoram, minced onion and parsley.
3. Cook it for another half hour and add a tablespoon of soda or catsup, and a tablespoon of flour mixed with cold water.
4. Let it boil up once and serve in a dish with onion on top.

title: Stewed String Beans (Grøntsagret Stillet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint shelled green peas
- 1 pint string beans
- a couple of carrots
- a couple of parsnips
- a small cauliflower head
- salad oil
- vinegar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- a little pepper

## Directions

1. Shell the peas. Use only the tender parts of a small bunch of asparagus. Cut the string beans into various shapes. Clean the carrots and parsnips and cut them into pieces about an inch in diameter. Cut the small cauliflower head into small florets.
2. Boil these ingredients and then place them in cold water so as not to lose their color. Boil a firm cauliflower head without breaking the florets, stirring the stem and root; when tender, peel and stir it like the carrots. Arrange the vegetables on a platter and dress with salad oil, vinegar, 1 tablespoon of salt and a little pepper.

title: Stiffelsbær Pie (Potato Starch Berry Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Butter
- 6 egg yolks
- a little finely grated vanilla
- powdered sugar to taste
- some old stale bread
- 2 small cupfuls of bread crumbs
- 3 tablespoons butter
- sifted berries

## Directions

1. Peel potatoes, cut into small pieces, and fry in butter until they become yellow, or give them a coating in butter and fry on a griddle until they are dry.
2. Beat 6 egg yolks well with a little finely grated vanilla and powdered sugar according to taste.
3. Soak some old stale bread; use 2 small cupfuls of the crumbs, which are boiled in 3 tablespoons butter, and when it has cooled slightly, stir it into the eggs together with the potato starch and the sifted berries.
4. Bake it in a tart tin.

title: Stiffelsbærfruktost til Steg (Crowberry Fruit Compote for Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- Unripe crowberries
- Water
- Butter
- Cinnamon
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Take unripe berries, but do not skin them first.
2. When stems and green nobs are removed, place them on the fire with very little water and a piece of unsalted butter the size of a walnut per quart of berries.
3. They should only be boiled tender, not mashed.
4. A few sticks of whole cinnamon are boiled with the berries, and sugar is added when the compote is boiled, but it should not be very sweet.

title: Stiffelsbærgrød (Crowberry Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- Crowberries
- Water
- Cornstarch
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Butter

## Directions

1. If the berries are unripe and very sour, let them stand an hour and draw in warm water, which is then poured off and put on the fire in fresh water.
2. For 2 quarts of berries, take 1/2 quart water.
3. Pick the berries and boil them out well and stir often from the bottom.
4. Then add the porridge thickened with sugar and cinnamon.
5. For crowberry porridge, coarse sugar can be used, but white sugar gives finer taste and more beautiful appearance.
6. One can also boil the berries without letting them be extracted first; use more water for the same portion of berries, and when the resulting porridge becomes too thin, thicken it with a couple spoonfuls of cornstarch stirred in cold water.
7. A third method is to take more berries and not let them boil quite to porridge, but to a gruel.
8. The pot is then put back on the fire, thickened with sugar and cinnamon, and finished with cornstarch.
9. Preserved crowberries in glasses can also be used for porridge, but always need thickening with cornstarch, as they lose their binding quality when preserved.

title: Stiftet Omelet (Whipped Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 egg yolks
- 6 tablespoons powdered sugar
- salt
- zest and juice of 1 lemon
- butter for greasing the pan

## Directions

1. Whip the 6 egg yolks for half an hour with 6 tablespoons powdered sugar, salt, and the zest and juice of the lemon, whisking two and stirring together carefully.
2. Grease the pan with butter, pour in the batter and bake in five minutes over moderate heat until it rises; then place it in a bed of coals and bake for an hour.
3. It cannot be taken from the pan and is served with hot butter.
4. This is an exceptionally fine and delicious omelet; but one must have good patience in stirring and whisking the eggs, as the goodness of it depends on this; otherwise it becomes heavy.

title: Stikkelsbærkage (Gooseberry Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 part unripe gooseberries
- 6 egg yolks
- ground cinnamon and sugar of rubber to taste
- some old white bread
- 2 small cups thereof
- 3 spoonfuls of butter

## Directions

1. 1 part unripe gooseberries are peeled, shelled and fried in the oven until they become yellow, or given a boiling in water and sieved on a sieve until they are dry.
2. 6 egg yolks are stirred well with a little ground cinnamon and rubber sugar to taste.
3. Some old white bread is grated, of which 2 small cups are used, which are fried in 3 spoonfuls of butter, and when it is a bit cooled, it is stirred into the eggs together with the gooseberries, and the beaten whites.
4. It is baked in an hour's time.

title: Stillet Høns (Breaded Chicken Pieces)
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat or chicken, preferably from the breast
- Beaten eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Butter
- Flour for dusting
- Semolina

## Directions

1. The meat or chicken is cut into quite thin slices and dipped in beaten eggs, then in bread crumbs mixed with salt, pepper and parsley, and fried quite hot in butter.
2. Served with semolina.

title: Strawberry Short-Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 short flour
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 scant cup sour milk
- 1 egg
- 4 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon baking soda dissolved in warm water
- half teaspoon salt
- salt for flour
- 2 tablespoons butter in small bits
- fresh strawberries
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Mix salt into flour, then work in butter in small bits like for pie, and blend with flour. Beat egg and soda into the milk. Mix everything together, handling as little as possible. Roll out in two layers of pie form but one inch thick. Lay one on top of the other and bake in a hot oven. While still warm but not hot, separate the layers. They will easily separate at the spot where they were joined. Spread a thick layer of fresh strawberries on the lower layer, dust with powdered sugar, and place the other layer on top. When serving, dust the top layer lightly with sugar.

title: Strawberry Soup (Jordbaersuppe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart strawberries
- ½ pint water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Hull and cook 1 quart strawberries in ½ pint water.
2. Cook until all the juice is drawn out of the berries.
3. Strain through a fine sieve and return to the heat with sugar, according to how sour the berries are; brown sugar may be used.
4. Set aside a little of the soup when it is done, and stir into it a heaping spoonful of cornstarch.
5. When the soup is near boiling, stir this mixture in, cook up again and it is ready to serve.
6. Serve with small rusks.
7. If it seems too thick, it can be thinned.

title: String Beans, Scandinavian Style (Knitlebønner)
body:
## Ingredients

- string beans
- butter
- wheat flour
- cream or milk
- minced parsley
- sugar
- nutmeg
- salt

## Directions

1. With the edge of a knife, take hold of the stem and you can then draw a long thread from it right up to the tip of the bean. This is called stringing the beans. Afterwards cut them diagonally, very fine; cook them tender in water without salt, then drain them dry in a sieve. They should not cook more than just tender, because they must not break. Melt butter, stir in wheat flour and thin it with cream or milk and let it boil to a smooth sauce. Afterwards the string beans get a seasoning with a little minced parsley, a piece of sugar, a little nutmeg and finally salt. About one good spoonful of butter, half a teaspoon of flour and a little pint of milk is approximately suitable for a bowl full of well-drained beans.

title: Strontiumssuppe (Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- peas
- water
- parsley
- bay leaves
- nutmeg
- cayenne
- carrots

## Directions

1. Make ordinary beef soup from beef
2. Add as many peas as will make the soup suitable
3. The peas should be like yellow varieties, which are somewhat thin
4. When peas are cooked in strong soup, especially if it is aged, it is best to remove some soup first before the meat is completely cooked
5. Mix with water and let the peas cook tender
6. Once the meat is cooked, strain the soup and set it on the fire where butter is melted so that you can press in almost a spoonful of flour, and when this is dissolved, dilute it first with water and then with soup
7. When all the soup has come in and is cooking, add fine parsley on top with the peas and their soup, nutmeg and a little cayenne
8. If you want young carrots, they are cooked whole with the peas
9. The soup can be made thinner with peas and with less seasoning, and add meatballs and carrots

title: Strontiumssuppe uden Kjød (Pea Soup without Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled and sliced peas
- 4 quarts water
- bushel of peas of average size
- a good and large turnip
- many carrots and parsnips
- 2½ ounces flour
- 3 ounces butter
- salt
- 1 spoonful sugar
- sorrel or parsley

## Directions

1. Place peeled and sliced peas in a pot with 4 quarts water for a bushel of peas of average size
2. Once they begin to cook, add a good and large turnip and many carrots and parsnips
3. When these are tender, remove them and cut into smaller pieces
4. Press the turnip well
5. Mix approximately 2½ ounces flour and 3 ounces butter together for seasoning and combine with the soup
6. Cook together with salt, 1 spoonful sugar, sorrel or parsley

title: Stuffed Artichokes (Tidsler)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- breadcrumbs
- fat
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- parsley
- onion
- egg
- butter
- cream
- minced parsley
- cornstarch

## Directions

1. Cook in ten minutes. Cut the plant open down the long sides, and pick out the kernels. Keep the opening open with the help of a piece of clean wood or other device, and place the plant in cold, salted water, while the filling is prepared as follows: Breadcrumbs, very small pieces of fat, salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, parsley and a very little minced onion are mixed finely together. This is stirred with a little cream and a beaten egg. With this fill the plant, bind it with soft twine to hold it together and bake it with a little hot water in the baking pan. When they begin to fry, baste them with butter and water; test with a straw, and when completely tender place them on a platter. Three spoonfuls of cream are added to the sauce which can also be thickened with a little cornstarch. A little minced parsley is put on, it boils just up and is held over the plant.

title: Stuffed Beef (Stegt Og Fogt Kylling)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Parsley
- Chives
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Bread
- Eggs
- Milk
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the meat and herbs (parsley and chives) into fine pieces and layer them on the meat
2. Alternatively, dip the meat in butter and clarified butter that has been melted on the stove, dust with bread and roast it
3. Serve with vegetables

title: Stuffed Onions (Meat-Stuffed Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- Large yellow onions
- ground meat
- water
- salt
- butter
- onion
- pepper
- cucumber or apple
- bouillon
- stock or soy

## Directions

1. When large yellow onions are peeled, a layer of them is cut, they are hollowed out, filled with ground meat, the layers are placed on top and they are tied together in a piece of thin cloth.
2. They are placed in boiling water and cooked until they are tender, then taken up, and the binding is loosened.
3. After this a brown sauce is made from browned butter, in which onion, pepper and cucumber or apple are cooked, which is mixed with bouillon, and a little stock or soy; the onions are given a quick boil in it or kept warm on a platter and covered with the sauce.
4. If you don't have enough onions, you can cook some carrots in the water at the same time as the onions and place them in the middle of the platter with the onions around them.

title: Stø Klappert (Stiff Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint sour milk
- 12 tablespoons flour
- 4 tablespoons soft rubber sugar

## Directions

1. 1 pint sour milk is whipped well and stirred with 12 tablespoons flour and 4 tablespoons soft rubber sugar
2. They are placed on the pan with a spoon in small strips and baked light brown
3. If the heat is good, full butter on the pan is needed for the first cakes

title: Sugar Omelette (Sukker Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- egg whites
- 1 tablespoon powdered sugar
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks and whites separately; instead of salt and pepper, add 1 tablespoon powdered sugar to the yolks
2. Cook in the same manner as Simple Omelette
3. While the omelette cooks, heat the serving dish until red-hot
4. When the omelette is slid onto the plate, sprinkle powdered sugar over it and score the top with a hot iron, taking care to brown the sugar rather than burn it
5. Serve hot

title: Suliennes Potatoes (Julienned Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Salt
- Water
- Fat

## Directions

1. Peel, pare into thin slices and soak in well salted, cold water for at least one hour.
2. Wash well in several waters and dry in a clean cloth.
3. Fry golden in hot fat, then place on gray paper to drain from fat and serve hot.

title: Sylteboller (Jam Buns)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint sour milk or buttermilk
- 2 beaten eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoonful soda
- 2 tablespoonfuls warm fat
- Flour

## Directions

1. Mix together 1 pint sour milk or buttermilk, 2 beaten eggs, 1 cup sugar, 1 teaspoonful soda, 2 tablespoonfuls warm fat
2. Then add as much flour as you find suitable thickness, so it can be handled

title: Syltede Ananas (Pineapple)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pineapple
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Peel the fruit, cut into slices and remove the core; then weigh it and take sugar and fruit in the proportion of one pound to one and a half pounds.
2. Place in preserving liquid, lay it out, and add for each pound of sugar a small cup of water.
3. When this is brought to boil, take out the fruit and spread it on dishes which are placed in the sun.
4. Cook the syrup and skim for half an hour; then add the fruit again and cook for fifteen minutes.
5. Take out the fruit, place in crocks and pour the boiling juice over; cover with a cloth until it is cold, then place brandy paper over and tie in the usual way.

title: Syltet Appelsin Skaltet (Candied Orange Peel)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples or oranges
- Sugar (pound for pound with fruit)
- Water

## Directions

1. The fruit is weighed whole and sugar is calculated pound for pound with the fruit.
2. The fruit is peeled neatly, and the peelings are cut into small strips.
3. They are cooked tender, and the water is changed twice, each time with warm water.
4. The juice from the fruit is poured over the sugar; this is brought to a boil, after which the strips are put in and it cooks for two minutes.
5. Lemon peel can be treated the same way, but more sugar should be calculated.

title: Syltet Hvidløg (Pickled Garlic)
body:
## Ingredients

- garlic cloves
- strong brine (salt water)

## Directions

1. Select fine, tender garlic cloves, not large ones. Let stand 4 days in strong brine, changing it twice. Peel the cloves before placing them in the brine. Bring the brine to a boil, add the cloves, and boil for 3 minutes. Then place them in cold water and leave for 3 hours. Pack them in crocks, layered with whole nutmeg, white peppercorns, and cloves. Fill with boiling vinegar in which 1 cup sugar per gallon of vinegar has been boiled. Bind while still warm. They can be used after a month, but are better later.

title: Syltet Ingefær (Candied Ginger)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh, slender ginger root
- Water
- Sugar (1 pound sugar and 1 cup water per 1 pound ginger)
- Soda solution (cold soda lye)

## Directions

1. Fresh ginger that is firm and smooth is beaten first for an hour, to see if it is dried out; such pieces are removed, but the good ones are weighed and put into cold water for 24 hours.
2. Then a cold soda lye is made and when the lye is warm, so it feels smooth under the fingers, the clear part is strained off, and in it lies the ginger until it is soft to feel; then it is rinsed for 8 days, and the water must be changed every day.
3. Now weigh the sugar: 1 pound sugar and 1 cup water per 1 pound ginger.
4. The sugar and water are cooked clear and skimmed, and then the ginger is cooked in it until it is so tender that it can be pierced with a fine straw.
5. It is stored when cool, but not bound over until it is cold.

title: Syltetøj af Ferskener (Preserved Peaches)
body:
## Ingredients

- Peaches, peeled and stoned
- For each pound of fruit: 1 pound sugar
- Kernels from a third of the stones
- Water for cooking the kernels

## Directions

1. Peel, slice and remove the stones from the fruit, using 1 pound sugar for each pound of fruit.
2. Crack a third of the stones, remove the kernels, cut into pieces and cook in barely boiling water until soft, then set aside on the stove covered.
3. In the bottom of a stone jar, place a layer of sugar, then a layer of fruit, continuing alternately until all is used, placing this on top of the pot so it will warm slowly until the sugar is melted and the fruit heated through.
4. Drain the water from the kernels and pour into the jar.
5. Let it cook gently until the fruit is tender and clear.
6. Carefully remove with a slotted spade and spread on flat plates, arranging so the pieces lie as little as possible on each other.
7. Boil the syrup until it is thick and even, and skim carefully.
8. Fill stone jars two-thirds full of fruit, and fill the remaining space with the boiled syrup.
9. Spread a paper over until it is cold, then cover with double paper moistened with brandy, then a cloth and finally with white paper.

title: Syltetøj af Melon (Preserved Melon)
body:
## Ingredients

- A melon that is not fully ripe
- For each pound of melon: 1 pound white sugar
- A third pint water

## Directions

1. Peel the melon, remove the seeds, and cut into finger-length pieces, approximately an inch wide and half as thick.
2. Weigh the pieces, place in a barrel that is so tight it can hold an egg floating in it, and let stand for 4 days.
3. Remove the pieces and place in cold water for 3 days, renewing the water each day.
4. Then place them on the stove in cold water and cook covered until so tender that a straw can be pierced through them.
5. Remove them and dry well in a cloth.
6. For each pound of melon, take 1 pound white sugar, which is boiled into a syrup in a third pint of water.
7. Add the melon pieces, boil for 5 to 6 minutes, pour the whole into a jar and let stand covered for 4 days.
8. Pour off the syrup, boil it for a few minutes and pour it warm over the melon, which stands for three more days.
9. Drain into a sieve, letting it stand some time for the juice to run off, then place on a plate.
10. Boil the syrup again until it is thick enough to pour over the melon.
11. Turn the melon around in it and let stand a few days in an airy place to dry.
12. Store in a dry place.

title: Søde Ferskener (Sweet Preserved Peaches) No. 1
body:
## Ingredients

- Peaches that are nearly ripe
- For each pound of peaches: 1 pound sugar
- For each pound of sugar: 1 pint white wine
- Water as needed

## Directions

1. Blanch the peaches and remove the pits.
2. Dip the sugar in water and boil into a syrup.
3. When the syrup is boiled, add to each pound of sugar 1 pint white wine.
4. Add the peaches, but only a few at a time, bring to a boil and carefully remove with a slotted spoon, placing on a cloth to drain.
5. When the syrup has drained, put it back in the pan to boil again.
6. Skim well, then add the kernels after cracking the stones and cutting them into pieces.
7. Place the peaches in jars and pour the syrup over them.
8. If the syrup becomes watery after a few days, boil again and pour cold over the peaches.

title: Søinelhovedet (Pig's Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's head
- Pig's feet
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Juniper berries
- Laurel leaf
- Cloves

## Directions

1. The head is placed in cold water.
2. After soaking overnight, it is washed well with a large cloth and placed over heat in cold water that stands over it.
3. Allow nearly 20 minutes per pound and it must not cook too quickly; it is skimmed regularly.
4. The skin should not be removed before the head is cold; then it comes off easily.
5. If the head is sent whole to the table, a piece of sturdy paper can be placed around the shank to hold it when carving.
6. It should be sliced very thinly.
7. The broth can be used for various purposes and no salt is used in it.

title: Tea Cream (The Creme)
body:
## Ingredients

- Strong extract of tea
- 10 egg yolks
- 1 quart cold cream
- 5 ounces sugar

## Directions

1. Make and serve it like the previous one, only using a strong extract of tea instead of coffee
2. It can be made without eggs, then taking for each portion less tea extract

title: Terrine of Meat Forcemeat (Beef Forcemeat Terrine)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ground meat
- butter
- greens
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Force is placed in an oval dish of either tin or lined copper, which is greased with butter.
2. In the middle a short hollow is made so that there is only about a hand's breadth of forcemeat all around on all sides.
3. This hollow is filled with finely chopped greens, which should not be too wet; force, as thin as a finger, is smeared over and sprinkled with butter; then it is placed in the oven on a trivet to cook through.
4. The oven must have heat like for sweet bread.
5. Of all kinds of greens, shelled green peas and white or green cabbage are best, because they are improved by the meat flavor and do not ruin it with their taste.
6. The peas should be cooked with a little stock or good broth.

title: Tomatkräm-Suppe (Tomato Cream Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart tomatoes
- 1 quart water
- 1 quart milk
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the tomatoes in water and let the milk become boiling hot over a boiling water bath; when the tomatoes are soft, add a tablespoon

title: Tomato Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe tomatoes
- salt
- mustard powder
- black pepper
- allspice
- cloves
- cayenne pepper
- white wine or cider vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Choose good ripe tomatoes
2. Pour them in boiling water and rub through a coarse sieve to free them from skin and seeds
3. When cooled, add 4 tablespoonfuls salt, 3 ounces ground mustard, 2 ounces black pepper, 1 ounce allspice, 1 ounce cloves, 1 ounce cayenne pepper and 1 pint white wine or cider vinegar to every gallon
4. Boil slowly for 4 hours, pour into bottles and seal well
5. Alternatively, for 1 peck of tomatoes take 6 pounds sugar (can be soft brown sugar) and 1 gallon good cider vinegar
6. Add cloves, cinnamon and pepper to taste
7. When the vinegar with sugar boils, add the spices and tomatoes gradually, letting them boil until a wooden spoon through them leaves no trace
8. They keep well in stone jars with good paper over them
9. Or pit and slice 1 pound ripe tomatoes; add 1.5 pounds sugar, 1 ounce cinnamon and mace blossoms mixed together, 1 ounce cloves and 1 quart vinegar
10. Combine all together and boil slowly for 1 hour

title: Tomato Sauce (Tomatoe Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pint of tomatoes
- large carrot
- onion
- salt pork
- bay leaf
- four cloves
- tablespoon of salt
- quart and a half of water or bouillon
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. In a small sauce pan or casserole put one pint of tomatoes, a large carrot and as much onion sliced in layers, furthermore a large piece of salt pork sliced into small pieces, a little bay leaf, four cloves, a tablespoon of salt, a quart and a half of water or bouillon, egg yolk or water. It is simmered slowly for one hour and then passed through a sieve with a spoon.

title: Tomatsuppe (Tomato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint tomatoes
- 2 quarts good broth or soup stock
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 tablespoon pepper
- 4 ounces rice, rinsed in cold water

## Directions

1. Cook the tomatoes until soft and strain through a sieve with a pestle
2. Stir them into 2 quarts broth and soup stock previously prepared; season with 1 tablespoon salt and 1 tablespoon pepper, then add 4 ounces of rice rinsed in cold water, and cook the soup slowly for half an hour before serving

title: Tærte (Tart)
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 egg yolks
- 1 pound powdered sugar
- egg whites beaten to stiff foam
- 2 ounces corn starch

## Directions

1. 10 egg yolks are stirred together with 1 pound powdered sugar.
2. The whites are beaten to stiff foam, mixed with 2 ounces corn starch and stirred carefully into the eggs, poured then into the form, which is lined with greased paper and baked a couple of times with little heat straight, later more.

title: Umobile Ferskener i Steg (Unripe Peaches in a Jar)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound unripe peaches, peeled
- 1 pound white sugar
- 1 gill vinegar
- A knob of white pint water

## Directions

1. Peel the peaches, divide into 4 parts and remove the stones.
2. Boil the sugar with the vinegar and a knob of white pint water.
3. When this is skimmed, add the peaches and cook for approximately 5 minutes (longer if very unripe).
4. Remove and place in a jar, boil the syrup a little longer, approximately 5 minutes, pour warm over and cover with a cloth.
5. After a few days, boil the syrup again for approximately 10 minutes, skim well, pour warm over and cover again with a cloth.
6. Finish as usual.

title: Unge Høet (Squabs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Squabs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and dry the squabs well with a clean cloth
2. They are best when fried in a pan, for which you brown sufficient butter
3. Fry them under a lid
4. When necessary, add a little water, but this must always be boiling

title: Vanilla Sauce (Vaniljesauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart cream
- 2 tablespoons vanilla
- 4 egg yolks
- A little white sugar

## Directions

1. Place 1 quart cream on heat with 2 tablespoons vanilla
2. When the cream boils, remove it and stir in 4 egg yolks that have first been stirred white with a little white sugar
3. Place it back on heat and stir constantly until it is just at boiling point and has become stiff
4. Then remove it, stir constantly until it is so cold that it no longer burns the skin, and pour it into a bowl for serving

title: Veal Strips (Kalvebiffsteg)
body:
## Ingredients

- tender veal
- flesh
- brown butter
- water or meat broth

## Directions

1. Cut the most tender veal into strips, 13 centimeters long, 2 fingers wide and somewhat broader than the thumb, though exact proportions are not critical
2. Beat the strips gently so they do not fall apart and season them well with flesh, leaving only one side uncovered
3. Brown butter in a shallow pan and brown the strips on all sides where they are seasoned, until the flesh is penetrated through
4. Turn the other side, which is raw, and when this is brown, add a little water or meat broth and cook on this side until thoroughly cooked
5. They are best when not overcooked but slightly juicy
6. When removed, stir a little flour into the sauce
7. You may also dip the strips in egg and pepper before seasoning, but then do not add flour to the sauce, only a piece of cold butter to finish it

title: Vegetables with Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- Vegetables (cut into thumb-length pieces)
- Veal or game stock
- Onion
- Tomatoes
- Sauce

## Directions

1. The vegetables are broken into thumb-long pieces and cooked until tender. The sauce is prepared beforehand. Half a pound of veal stock is cut in strips, placed in cold water and cooked for half an hour. Then a minced onion and a pint of tomatoes, peeled and cut in slices, are added. It cooks for an hour, then the meat is removed and the rest remains. In all, only a pint of sauce is needed for a short dish of macaroni.

title: Vivid Nova of Pickled Vegetables
body:
## Ingredients

- meat, bones and juice from cucumbers used for pickles
- salt
- vinegar
- various herbs and spices

## Directions

1. Put all the meat, bones and juice extracted from the cucumbers used for pickles on the fire with a little salt and boil for about 1 hour, stirring constantly, so nothing sticks, which would give a burned or boiled taste; if this happens, it must be discarded
2. Strain through a sieve
3. Put the juice back on the fire with 1 gill vinegar to 1 pint juice and boil until thick, about 1 hour
4. Then cool it, and the next day pour into small bottles or glasses and tie well
5. This sauce gives a pleasant taste to all kinds of white sauces, fricassees and the like, and should be added sparingly so it boils a little with the sauce

title: Wheat Flour Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint flour
- 1 pint milk
- 1 good quart flour
- 6 eggs
- 1 tablespoon butter and an equal amount fat, melted together

## Directions

1. Whip the eggs very well, yolks and whites separately
2. Then add the milk with a little salt, then the butter and finally the flour, which is stirred lightly in
3. Bake immediately in well-buttered rings that are half-filled with batter
4. The oven must be very hot and the baker must watch so that the edges brown well

title: Wheat Groat Porridge Cakes (Hvede-Fladekager)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter, melted
- 1 pound wheat flour
- Nearly 3 quarts boiling water
- Salt
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and bake 1 pound wheat flour in it until it slips easily from the spoon.
2. Bit by bit pour nearly 3 quarts of boiling water onto it, so the porridge boils well between each addition and with strong stirring so it does not become lumpy.
3. It becomes very thin from this amount, but it thickens at the finishing, which must be done as soon as it is cooked; add salt.
4. Instead of baking the porridge whole, it can after it is prepared be fried on the griddle in small flat cakes.
5. These are eaten warm with sugar, butter, stewed apples or strawberries.

title: Wilde Fugle (Wild Birds)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young fresh game bird
- Paper
- Water
- Soda
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bread stuffing or egg stuffing

## Directions

1. A fresh game bird is young and fresh, which is the main condition for it to become well-cooked.
2. Pluck the feathers, remove the down and hairs by rubbing them well with a piece of paper so that nothing dark is left on them.
3. Cut the birds in the following manner: cut a slit along the backbone and pull it out boneless, and large enough that a hand can pass through it, and remove the contents.
4. Remove the crop, which lies at the bird's gullet boundary.
5. The entire cleaning is done in lukewarm water and must be very careful; in the next water add a piece of soda; let cold water run through the bird many times, and when you are completely certain of the cleaning, dry it well inside with a clean cloth.
6. The head and bones below the ankle are cut off and not used.
7. The gizzard, heart and liver, when cleaned, can be used in various ways; either minced and added to the filling or in the sauce, or one can simply put them inside the whole bird.
8. On hens and chickens the breastbone can be cut inside to give them a round shape, but on game birds it must be done from the outside.
9. One can have a bread dough and stick it between the loose skin and the meat; it gives the breast a beautiful golden appearance.
10. To prevent the bones from sticking out when the bird is cooked, you can cut a hole fairly high up on each side of the cut skin, push the hairs back so that the tendons can be pulled into the hole and tied around them.
11. On ducks and geese the breastbone is not cut.
12. One should never use a fork to turn the birds—because precious juice runs out—but use a clean wooden spoon.
13. One counts generally 15 minutes of roasting for every pound the bird weighs.
14. Wash the game bird in cold water.
15. If you prefer, you can singe it.
16. To fill it around where the crop has been; for this use is allowed a piece of a few ounces of white bread that is soaked in milk and then stands covered for an hour.
17. A piece of cold butter is stirred until smooth, then the bread is stirred, after being moistened into pieces with a spoon, salt, a little sweet ginger, a pair of cloves and 3 eggs.
18. When it is well mixed, one can add the gizzard, liver and heart, which are finely minced, fill it in the skin and sew it, but the filling must not push down into the game bird, so a piece of white bread must be placed in the internal hole below the neck.
19. The oven must be well heated before the game bird is put in, and it must have an even warmth, but not too strong, as it would otherwise become too dark brown.
20. It cannot be roasted in a pot when it is filled.
21. It is placed in with water and a little salt, by which it is gradually basted; later butter is laid on it, by which it is then basted.
22. The best filling for a duck is apples and good prunes, it gives the most pleasant flavor; but others allow a dough of eggs, bread and milk and fill with it.
23. One can also just rub it inside with salt and pepper and then place a piece of juniper wood inside; these are completely consumed during roasting, but they give the duck a fresh flavor.
24. It is placed in the oven with a little water on it, which soon becomes fat, by which the duck is often basted.
25. As sauce sharp red wine sauce can be used, but generally only sauce made in the usual way is used.
26. Fat should be well skimmed off before the sauce is made.

title: Young Peas (Unge Søg)
body:
## Ingredients

- young peas
- water
- milk
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. They must be cooked this way: They are shelled, placed in cold water, and then in a saucepan with boiling water. When they are half done, all the water is poured off, except a little cupful; to this is added an equal amount of milk and a large spoonful of butter. It simmers until the peas are completely tender, after which they are arranged with a little salt and pepper, as desired.

title: Ål à la Chunt (Eel à la Chunt)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 eel
- salt
- water
- 1 ounce flour
- 1 quart or good veal stock
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 bunch chopped parsley
- 1 finely chopped onion
- salt
- pepper
- 0.5 pounds butter
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. After cleaning the eel, rub it with salt and place it whole in boiling water that completely covers it, and set it over the fire.
2. When it boils, place it at the back of the oven where it can simmer gently; let it cook for one hour, then remove it and pull out all the bones.
3. In a saucepan, blend one ounce of flour with a quart or good veal stock, which is added little by little.
4. Then add the juice of one lemon, a bunch of chopped parsley, one finely chopped onion and a little salt and pepper.
5. Set this over the fire and stir until it becomes a thick sauce, then add 0.5 pounds of butter.
6. Bring the whole to a boil, then strain it.
7. Pour a little of the sauce onto a deep dish, place the eel in it and pour the rest of the sauce over it.
8. Whip the whites of 6 eggs into a foam, spread it over the top and bake the whole until brown in the oven.

title: Æbel Dumplings (Apple Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- dough prepared as for pie dough but with milk instead of water
- a good peeled apple with the core removed
- for each apple: salt and sugar or sugar with lemon juice mixed together

## Directions

1. The dough is rolled out in a thin sheet
2. It is cut into squares, and on each a good peeled apple is placed with the core removed
3. The hollow where the core was is filled with either salted or sugar mixed with lemon juice, with a lump of sugar placed in the sugar
4. The dough is bent together around the apple, tied in a cloth with enough room left for the pudding to rise, and cooked for one hour
5. The water must be boiling and not off the boil
6. The cloth is opened in warm water and melted inside before using

title: Æblegrød (Apple Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- Whole apples
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Apples can be made from whole apples that are first peeled.
2. They are boiled in water or steamed; as the apples are peeled, they are placed in water to prevent browning.
3. This water is discarded.
4. When the apples are tender, rub them through a coarse sieve so that the core remains behind.
5. Place them back on the fire to be thickened with sugar.
6. Alternatively, cut the apples into small pieces after they are peeled and have soaked in cold water, and place them on the fire with little water according to how liquid the apples are, and let them boil quite gently; they should boil under a covered lid, and you should not stir until they are fully cooked.
7. Then stir very gently, and white sugar is added according to the apples' sweetness.
8. Stir quickly during the last cooking the porridge now gets, and it is kept to be eaten cold with sugar and cream.
9. It is not correct to use any kind of thickening for apple porridge, but should you happen to make it too thin, there is nothing for it but to take a couple spoonfuls of cornstarch stirred in a little cold water, which is then added after the sugar; it should only boil a couple minutes.
10. As you do not willingly stir in this porridge before it is fully cooked, it is somewhat liable to scorch; this is avoided best when, while the porridge is clean and dry, you smear it inside with soft butter and cover it with cold water.
11. Of course you should not place it over the fire.

title: Æbler (Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 quart water
- 1 pair stick cinnamon
- 1 pound white sugar
- 1 pint of the water in which apples are cooked
- A little lemon juice

## Directions

1. The apples must be good and juicy. Scrape, cut into 4 pieces, remove the core and weigh them.
2. Place 1 pound butter on fire with 1 quart water and 1 pair stick cinnamon; when it boils, add the apples, and when they have had 2 boils place them on a sieve so the water can drain.
3. Cook 1 pound white sugar into syrup with 1 pint of the water in which the apples are cooked; add these along with a little lemon juice, and cook very gently until they are clear, usually in 5 minutes; then remove from fire; the apples remain a little in it, whereupon they are taken out and placed in the crock.
4. If the juice is not thick when it is cold, it must be cooked a little more and poured cold over the apples, which are tied the next day.
5. These preserved apples can only keep a couple of months.

title: Æbler I (Apples I)
body:
## Ingredients

- Good, tender apples
- Sugar
- Wine

## Directions

1. Peel and divide into 2 or 4 pieces.
2. They get a boiling in sour wine and sugar.
3. Then cook the white sugar clear and thick and pour lukewarm over the apples; the wine is not used, but can be used for soup.
4. They should not be placed in a narrow crock the first time, but the sauce should be poured over them in a wide dish where they do not lie much on top of each other.
5. Two days later they can be placed in the crock.
6. The sauce gets a boiling and is poured cold over.
7. They cannot be kept as long as other preserved sweet dishes.

title: Æblerne Milfeuille (Apples Millefeuille)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine apples
- sugar
- water
- a piece of savory pie without juice or a piece of raspberry or cherry marmalade
- the remaining pieces of bread
- a custard made from a quart of milk
- 8 egg yolks
- a little vanilla
- a tablespoon cornstarch
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Peel fine apples, cut off a piece, hollow them out, and give them a filling of sugar and a little water, but so they do not fall apart.
2. Place them beside each other in a fairly deep dish filled with the savory pie without juice or with a piece of raspberry or cherry marmalade, and cover with the remaining pieces of bread.
3. Make a custard of a quart of milk, 8 egg yolks, a little vanilla, a tablespoon cornstarch, and sugar to taste.
4. When the custard is cold, pour it over the apples, place the dish in the oven, and bake the cake slowly.
5. When the custard begins to stiffen and has become light brown, it is done.

title: Æblerne Omslynget (Apples Enveloped)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- stale bread or rusk bread
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- cherry or other fruit juice or water

## Directions

1. Peel and slice the apples into not too thin pieces; remove the inner cores, and it does not matter if the slices are not uniform.
2. Grate stale bread or rusk bread finely; put a little butter in a bowl, which should be a stoneware vessel, place it on the stove until the butter is melted and spread the bowl around so the butter goes all over.
3. First spread a fairly thick layer of bread in the bowl and over it lay small pieces of butter at regular intervals; over this lay a layer of apples, as close together as possible, and over this sprinkle powdered sugar according to the apples' sourness and a little cinnamon; when this is done, pour cherry or other fruit juice around; then begin again with a good layer of bread and proceed thus; the top layer must be bread and then spread well with butter all around and finally pour the juice over it again.
4. Bake it in a moderately warm oven; when the apples can be pierced through without resistance, the cake is done.
5. It can become excellent without juice; then nothing is put inside the bowl instead of juice, but when the bowl is full, a little water is poured over it.

title: Æbleskiver (Apple Slices)
body:
## Ingredients

- Apple slices
- Sugar
- Horseradish (scraped)
- Butter (small pieces)

## Directions

1. Weigh the whole apples and calculate pound for pound of sugar with the fruit.
2. The fruit is peeled neatly, and the peelings are cut into small strips.
3. They are cooked tender, and the water is changed twice, each time with warm water.
4. The juice from the apples is poured over the sugar; this is brought to a boil, after which the strips are put in and it cooks for two minutes.

title: Éclairs (Éclairs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound flour baked in 1/2 pound clarified butter
- 1 pint boiling water
- 10 eggs
- vanilla

## Directions

1. The dough is made of 1/2 pound flour which is baked well in 1/2 pound clarified butter; then 1 pint boiling water is added and when it is baked with it, it is cooled a little, stirred for half an hour with 10 eggs, one at a time and placed half in small forms, but in lack thereof with a spoon in small strips on the pan
2. From this become 25 cakes
3. They can be filled with vanilla cream or whipped cream

title: Øllebrød-Suppe (Beer Bread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart water
- 1 teaspoon of butter
- 1 pint milk
- 2 tablespoons coarse salt
- 0.5 tablespoon pepper
- 1 quart ale

## Directions

1. Take 1 quart water, 1 teaspoon of butter, 1 pint milk, 2 tablespoons coarse salt and 0.5 tablespoon pepper; let the whole come to a quick boil and then add 1 quart ale, let it boil quickly up and then set aside from the stove

title: Ørredssuppe (Trout Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound beef
- 1 onion
- 3 carrots
- 3 quarts water
- turnip
- meatballs
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cut 1 pound beef very finely and cook it slowly with 1 onion and 3 carrots in 3 quarts water until the meat can be pulled apart, which takes almost 3 hours
2. For the soup not to boil away, it is necessary that it cooks gently in a pot with a tightly fitting lid
3. A pair of turnips and meatballs are added and removed when they are tender
4. Strain the soup, set it back on the fire, skim it, and when it boils, add meatballs made from ½ pound beef
5. The soup can be seasoned with 1 egg and served with toasted bread
6. This portion is for 3 people

title: Østers "on the Half-Shell" (Oysters on the Half-Shell)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters
- Water
- Milk
- Cream
- Butter
- Salt
- White pepper
- Corn starch

## Directions

1. Best prepared in a special oyster boiler, but in its absence, place a smaller pan inside a larger one with boiling water
2. In the smaller pan put one cup warm water, one cup milk, one cup cream
3. When it boils, stir in two spoonfuls of butter, as well as a little salt and white pepper
4. Remove from heat and bind with two heaping spoonfuls of corn starch stirred in a little cold milk
5. Meanwhile, the oyster shells should be washed and buttered with butter and an intact oyster laid on each
6. Place these close together next to the edge of the frying pan; stir the cream very well, and fill each shell up, being careful not to spill anything in the pan
7. Bake 5 to 9 minutes, after the shell has become warm
8. Arrange on the shells
9. One can use oyster stock instead of water and milk instead of cream

title: Østers i Casserole (Oysters in Casserole)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bread crumbs
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Butter a casserole dish and line the sides and all corners with bread dough
2. The oysters should be as large and fine as possible; drain all liquid from them
3. Season the oysters with pepper, salt and butter
4. Lay them on the pan with as much of their own sauce as desired, and sprinkle three hard-boiled, finely chopped egg yolks and raw bread over
5. Roll a layer toward the edge and press the edge in scallops
6. Bake with brisk heat
7. One can also add a half pint of cream with a little corn starch stirred in

title: Østers Panerede (Breaded Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters
- Butter
- Egg
- Bread crumbs or crackers
- Lemon slices

## Directions

1. For this use the largest and best oysters; lay them on a clean cloth and press another cloth over them to absorb all moisture
2. In the frying pan, melt butter enough to almost cover the oysters
3. First dip them in beaten egg and then roll in soft bread or crackers
4. Fry them light brown
5. Serve dry on a hot pan
6. Lemon slices can be arranged around them

title: Østers à la Crème (Oysters with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Oysters
- Butter
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cream or milk
- Corn starch

## Directions

1. Drain the liquid from two quarts of good oysters into a saucepan and cook until the milk begins to separate out
2. Strain the oysters; put the liquid over heat, and when it is near boiling, bind it with corn starch stirred into a little cold milk
3. Add the oysters and cook for five minutes
4. Serve hot with toast
5. The sauce is made from two quarts of good oyster liquid, blended with a little hot water, a little salt and pepper and poured over the side
6. When it is near boiling, add a large cup of cream or good milk
7. When it comes to a boil, add the oysters and they boil for about five minutes
8. When they shrivel, add two spoonfuls of butter and when it is melted, remove the pan from the heat
9. Arrange quickly with oyster crackers
10. Oysters become flavorless if they are cooked too long or if too long after being cooked
